title.raw: 卷一下 光武帝紀 Volume 1b: Annals of Emperor Guangwu

sentences.zh: 六年春正月丙辰，改舂陵郷為章陵縣。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, on bingchen in the first month of spring, Chunling township was changed to Zhangling county.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year, on the bingchen day in early spring, the court redesignated Chunling township as Zhangling county.

sentences.zh: 世世復傜役，比豐、沛，無有所豫。

translations.literal: For every generation corvée labor was remitted, on the same footing as Feng and Pei, with nothing held back.
translations.idiomatic: Corvée duties were waived for every generation, on the same generous terms granted to Gaozu’s home districts of Feng and Pei, and no further levy was to be imposed.

sentences.zh: 〈高祖豐沛邑人，故代代復，今比之也。

translations.literal: 〈The Gaozu came from the people of Feng and Pei, hence exemption was granted generation after generation; now it is equated to that.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〈Gaozu’s clansmen in Feng and Pei had enjoyed hereditary exemption from corvée; Guangwu’s Chunling kindred were now given the same privilege.〉

sentences.zh: 復音福。〉

translations.literal: 〈The gloss notes that the character here is read with the same sound as fu (blessing).〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The commentary indicates this graph is pronounced like fu, “blessing,” in the sense of remission.〉

sentences.zh: 辛酉，詔曰：「往歲水旱蝗蟲為灾，穀價騰躍，

translations.literal: On xinyou an edict said: “In past years flood, drought, and locusts brought disaster, and grain prices shot up.
translations.idiomatic: On the xinyou day the emperor issued an edict: “Flood, drought, and locusts in recent years had ruined the harvests, and the price of grain had soared.

sentences.zh: 〈言踴貴也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Meaning that it became dear.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〈The commentator glosses the phrase as ‘became exorbitantly expensive.’〉

sentences.zh: 人用困乏。

translations.literal: The people were exhausted and destitute.
translations.idiomatic: The common people were worn down and left without resources.

sentences.zh: 朕惟百姓無以自贍，惻然愍之。

translations.literal: I reflect that the hundred surnames have no way to support themselves; I am deeply moved and pity them.
translations.idiomatic: He reflected that ordinary folk had no means to keep themselves fed, and his heart went out to them.

sentences.zh: 其命郡國有穀者，給稟

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that wherever in commanderies and kingdoms there is grain, rations be issued
translations.idiomatic: He ordered every commandery and kingdom that held grain in store to issue relief rations

sentences.zh: 〈說文：「稟，賜穀也。

translations.literal: 〈The Shuowen says: “The graph read bin here means grain given as a grant.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Shuowen defines the word used for relief grain as a general term for distributed rations.〉

sentences.zh: 」音筆錦反。〉

translations.literal: 〉Pronounced with the fanqie bi jin.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The gloss gives the fanqie reading bi–jin.〉

sentences.zh: 高年、鰥、寡、孤、獨及篤［］、無家屬貧不能自存者，如律。

translations.literal: The very old, widowers, widows, orphans, those living alone, the gravely infirm, and those without family who are too poor to survive on their own shall be treated according to statute.
translations.idiomatic: Anyone over the usual age threshold, widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, the seriously ill without kin, and the destitute who could not subsist were to receive aid under the standing rules.

sentences.zh: 〈大戴禮曰：「六十無妻曰鰥，五十無夫曰寡。

translations.literal: 〈The Da Dai li says: “Sixty without a wife is called guan; fifty without a husband is called gua.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Da Dai li defines guan as a widower at sixty and gua as a widow at fifty, using those classical labels.〉

sentences.zh: 」禮記曰：「幼而無父曰孤，老而無子曰獨。

translations.literal: 〉The Liji says: “Young without a father is called gu; old without a son is called du.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Liji adds gu for a fatherless child and du for a childless elder, the standard ritual vocabulary.〉

sentences.zh: 」爾雅曰：「篤，困也。

translations.literal: 〉The Erya says: “Du (the graph used here for grave infirmity) means pressed, in straits.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Erya glosses the word as “in distress,” explaining the category of the seriously ailing.〉

sentences.zh: 」蒼頡篇曰：「［］，病也。

translations.literal: 〉The Cangjie pian says: ‘[missing graph] means illness.’
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Cangjie pian supplies a character for serious illness (the manuscript is defective in the received text).〉

sentences.zh: 」漢律今亡。〉

translations.literal: 〉The Han statutes are lost today.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Han legal provisions cited here no longer survive.〉

sentences.zh: 二千石勉加循撫，無令失職。」

translations.literal: The officials at two thousand shi shall diligently go among them with comfort and care, and must not let them fall out of their proper livelihood.”
translations.idiomatic: Magistrates ranked at two thousand shi were to tour their jurisdictions, soothe the people, and see that none were left without the means to live.”

sentences.zh: 〈職猶常也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Here zhi means their regular livelihood or condition.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉In this edict “losing office” means losing the means of subsistence, not only a post title.〉

sentences.zh: 揚武將軍馬成等拔舒，獲李憲。

translations.literal: Generals Ma Cheng styled Yangwu and others took Shu and captured Li Xian.
translations.idiomatic: Yangwu general Ma Cheng and his colleagues captured Shu and took the rebel leader Li Xian.

sentences.zh: 二月，大司馬吳漢拔朐，獲董憲、龐萌，山東悉平。

translations.literal: In the second month Grand Marshal Wu Han took Qu, captured Dong Xian and Pang Meng, and all east of the mountains was pacified.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month Wu Han, grand marshal, seized Qu, seized Dong Xian and Pang Meng, and the entire Shandong region was brought to heel.

sentences.zh: 諸將還京師，置酒賞賜。

translations.literal: The generals returned to the capital; a banquet was held and rewards were distributed.
translations.idiomatic: The victorious commanders came back to Luoyang, where the court feasted them and handed out honors.

sentences.zh: 三月，公孫述遣將任滿寇南郡。

translations.literal: In the third month Gongsun Shu sent the general Ren Man to raid Nan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Gongsun Shu’s general Ren Man struck south into Nan commandery.

sentences.zh: 〈今荊州也。〉

translations.literal: 〈That is the present Jingzhou region.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〈Nan commandery corresponds roughly to the Jingzhou area.〉

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙子，幸長安，始謁高廟，遂有事十一陵。

translations.literal: In summer, on bingzi in the fourth month, he arrived at Chang’an, first paid homage at the shrine to the High Ancestor, then performed the sacrifices at the eleven imperial tombs.
translations.idiomatic: That summer, on bingzi in the fourth month, he reached Chang’an, worshipped at Gaozu’s temple, and carried out the full round of offerings at eleven Western Han mausolea.

sentences.zh: 〈有事謂祭也。

translations.literal: 〈“You shi” in annals usage means to perform a sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The phrase refers to holding a scheduled offering, not ordinary business.〉

sentences.zh: 左傳曰：「有事於太廟。

translations.literal: The Zuozhuan says: ‘There was a sacrifice in the Grand Temple.’
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Zuozhuan uses the same wording for rites at the dynastic temple.〉

sentences.zh: 」高祖長陵，惠帝安陵，文帝霸陵，景帝陽陵，武帝茂陵，昭帝平陵，宣帝杜陵，元帝渭陵，成帝延陵，哀帝義陵，平帝康陵。〉

translations.literal: 〉The High Ancestor’s Changling, Emperor Hui’s Anling, Emperor Wen’s Baling, Emperor Jing’s Yangling, Emperor Wu’s Maoling, Emperor Zhao’s Pingling, Emperor Xuan’s Duling, Emperor Yuan’s Weiling, Emperor Cheng’s Yanling, Emperor Ai’s Yiling, Emperor Ping’s Kangling.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The eleven tombs are those of Western Han emperors from Gaozu through Ping, each named with its hill-tomb.〉

sentences.zh: 遣虎牙大將軍蓋延等七將軍從隴道伐公孫述。

translations.literal: He sent Grand General Gai Yan styled Huya and six other generals to march against Gongsun Shu by the Long road.
translations.idiomatic: Huya general Gai Yan and six fellow commanders were ordered up the Longshan route to attack Gongsun Shu.

sentences.zh: 五月己未，至自長安。

translations.literal: On jiwei in the fifth month he returned from Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei in the fifth month he came back from Chang’an to the eastern capital.

sentences.zh: 隗嚻反，蓋延等因與嚻戰於隴阺，諸將敗績。

translations.literal: Wei Xiao rebelled; Gai Yan and the others therefore fought Wei Xiao at Longdi, and the generals suffered a rout.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao rose in revolt, and where Gai Yan engaged him at Longdi the imperial army was thrown back in defeat.

sentences.zh: 辛丑，詔曰：「惟天水、隴西、安定、北地

translations.literal: On xinchou an edict said: “As for the commanderies of Tianshui, Longxi, Anding, and Beidi
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou the emperor proclaimed: “In the commanderies of Tianshui, Longxi, Anding, and Beidi

sentences.zh: 〈並郡名。

translations.literal: 〈These are all commandery names.
translations.idiomatic: 〉These are four northwestern commanderies.〉

sentences.zh: 天水今秦州，安定今涇州，北地今寧州，隴西今渭州。〉

translations.literal: 〉Tianshui is modern Qinzhou, Anding modern Jingzhou, Beidi modern Ningzhou, Longxi modern Weizhou.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The commentary maps each name to a Tang-Song prefecture roughly on the old ground.〉

sentences.zh: 吏人為隗嚻所詿誤者，

translations.literal: officials and commoners who were misled into error by Wei Xiao,
translations.idiomatic: civil officers and commoners who had been duped into following Wei Xiao,

sentences.zh: 〈說文曰：「詿亦誤也。

translations.literal: 〈The Shuowen says: “The graph read gui here also means to err or mislead.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Shuowen glosses it as “to lead into error,” the sense intended for people misled by Wei Xiao.〉

sentences.zh: 」音古賣反。〉

translations.literal: 〉Pronounced gu mai (fanqie).〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The reading is gu–mai by fanqie.〉

sentences.zh: 又三輔遭難赤眉，有犯法不道者，

translations.literal: and those of the three metropolitan areas who suffered at the hands of the Red Eyebrows and committed offenses that were not ‘non-dao’ (inhuman cruelty),
translations.idiomatic: as well as people of the three Fu districts who, during the Red Eyebrow turmoil, had broken laws short of the capital crime of ‘inhuman cruelty,’

sentences.zh: 〈前書音義曰：「律：殺不辜一家三人為不道。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Yin Yi on the Former Han says: “By statute, killing three innocent members of one family counts as budao (inhuman cruelty).”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Budao is the Former Han legal label for a particularly heinous pattern of homicide.〉

sentences.zh: 自殊死以下，皆赦除之。」

translations.literal: from death by dismemberment downward, all are pardoned and cleared.”
translations.idiomatic: every penalty short of execution was to be remitted.”

sentences.zh: 六月辛卯，詔曰：「夫張官置吏，所以為人也。

translations.literal: On xinmao in the sixth month an edict said: “Setting up offices and appointing clerks is done for the sake of the people.
translations.idiomatic: On xinmao in the sixth month an edict declared: “Bureaucracies exist for the people’s benefit.

sentences.zh: 〈管子曰：「張官置吏，所以奉主之法。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Guanzi says: ‘Setting up offices and appointing clerks is how one upholds the ruler’s laws.’〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Guanzi makes the same point about government serving the law and the people.〉

sentences.zh: 今百姓遭難，戶口耗少，而縣官吏職所置尚繁，其令司隷、州牧

translations.literal: Now the hundred surnames have met with disaster, household registers are depleted, yet the offices and posts of counties are still numerous; let the metropolitan intendant and the provincial governors
translations.idiomatic: Yet after years of war the population had shrunk while county government remained overstaffed; the emperor therefore told the metropolitan intendant and the provincial governors

sentences.zh: 〈漢官儀曰：「司隷校尉部河南、河內、右扶風、左馮翊、京兆、河東、弘農七郡於河南洛陽，故謂東京為『司隷』。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Han guan yi says: ‘The colonel director of retainers supervised Henan, Henei, You Fufeng, Zuo Pingyi, Jingzhao, Hedong, and Hongnong—seven commanderies from his seat at Luoyang in Henan, hence the eastern capital was called “under the colonel director of retainers.”’〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The note explains why Luoyang administration was nicknamed ‘Si zhi’ after the colonel’s seven-commandery jurisdiction.〉

sentences.zh: 各實所部，省減吏員。

translations.literal: each verify their jurisdictions and reduce the number of clerks.
translations.idiomatic: to audit their regions and cut redundant clerical posts.

sentences.zh: 縣國不足置長吏可并合者，

translations.literal: Where a county or fief is too small to justify a chief magistrate and may be merged,
translations.idiomatic: Any county or marquisate so small that its magistracy could be combined with another,

sentences.zh: 〈并音必政反。〉

translations.literal: 〈The graph in “merge” is read with the fanqie bing–zheng.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The commentary supplies the fanqie reading for the graph used in county mergers.〉

sentences.zh: 上大司徒、大司空二府。

translations.literal: report it up to the great minister of education and the great minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: was to be reported to the two high ministers for consolidation.

sentences.zh: 」於是條奏并省四百餘縣，吏職減損，十置其一。

translations.literal: ”Thereupon memorials listed mergers and abolitions of more than four hundred counties; official posts were cut back until roughly one in ten of the former number remained.
translations.idiomatic: Memorials followed, merging or abolishing over four hundred counties and slashing appointments to about a tenth of the old establishment.

sentences.zh: 代郡太守劉興擊盧芳將賈覽於高柳，戰歿。

translations.literal: Liu Xing, grand administrator of Dai commandery, attacked Lu Fang’s general Jia Lan at Gaoliu and died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: The grand administrator of Dai, Liu Xing, struck Lu Fang’s officer Jia Lan at Gaoliu and was killed in the fighting.

sentences.zh: 〈高柳，縣，屬代郡，故城在今雲州定襄縣。〉

translations.literal: 〈Gaoliu was a county in Dai commandery; its old walled town lies in what is now Dingxiang county in Yun prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Gaoliu lay in northern Dai near modern Shanxi borderlands.〉

sentences.zh: 初，樂浪人王調據郡不服。

translations.literal: At first Wang Diao, a man of Lelang, had held the commandery and refused submission.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier a Lelang native, Wang Diao, had seized the commandery and defied the court.

sentences.zh: 〈樂浪，郡，故朝鮮國也，在遼東。〉

translations.literal: 〈Lelang commandery was the old state of Korea, east of the Liao region.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Lelang was the Han commandery on the old Wiman Korean heartland.〉

sentences.zh: 秋，遣樂浪太守王遵擊之，郡吏殺調降。

translations.literal: In autumn the court sent Wang Zun, grand administrator of Lelang, against him; commandery clerks killed Wang Diao and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn Wang Zun marched east as grand administrator of Lelang; local officials assassinated Wang Diao and opened the gates.

sentences.zh: 遣前將軍李通率二將軍，與公孫述將戰於西城，破之。

translations.literal: The former general Li Tong leading two other generals fought Gongsun Shu’s officers at Xicheng and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Former general Li Tong, at the head of two allied commands, met Gongsun Shu’s army at Xicheng and broke it.

sentences.zh: 〈西城，縣，屬漢中，今金州縣也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Xicheng was a county in Hanzhong; it is modern Jinzhou county.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Xicheng lay in southern Hanzhong near the Ba–Han corridor.〉

sentences.zh: 夏，蝗。

translations.literal: In summer there were locusts.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts swarmed that summer.

sentences.zh: 秋九月庚子，赦樂浪謀反大逆殊死已下。

translations.literal: On gengzi in the ninth month of autumn an amnesty was proclaimed for Lelang for plotting rebellion, great treason, and all capital crimes short of the very worst.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in autumn an edict forgave everyone in Lelang guilty of rebellion or capital treason down to ordinary capital offenses.

sentences.zh: 丙寅晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On bingyin, the last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The month ended on bingyin with an eclipse of the sun.

sentences.zh: 冬十月丁丑，詔曰：「吾德薄不明，寇賊為害，彊弱相陵，元元失所。

translations.literal: On dingchou in the tenth month of winter an edict said: “My virtue is slight and my understanding dim; bandits and invaders do harm, the strong bully the weak, and the common people lose their homes.
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou in the tenth month he admitted: “My moral power is thin and my judgment clouded; rebels ravage the land, the mighty trample the weak, and families are driven from their homes.

sentences.zh: 詩云：『日月告凶，不用其行。』

translations.literal: The Classic of Poetry says: ‘The sun and moon announce ill fortune; they do not keep to their courses.’
translations.idiomatic: The Shijing warns: ‘Sun and moon proclaim disaster; they no longer run true courses.’

sentences.zh: 〈詩小雅鄭玄注云：「告凶，告天下凶亡之徵也。

translations.literal: 〈Zheng Xuan’s note on the Xiao ya says: “Announcing ill fortune means proclaiming omens of ruin for the realm.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉Zheng Xuan reads the ode’s phrase as heaven proclaiming dynastic crisis through the luminaries.〉

sentences.zh: 行，道度也。

translations.literal: “Xing” here means the measured paths or orbits.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The gloss takes xing as the regular tracks of sun and moon across the sky.〉

sentences.zh: 不用之者，謂相干犯。」〉

translations.literal: “Not keeping to them” means they cross and violate one another.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The line means the celestial bodies encroach on one another, as in eclipse, disturbing the cosmic order.”〉

sentences.zh: 永念厥咎，內疚於心。

translations.literal: I long ponder this fault and feel sick at heart within.
translations.idiomatic: He brooded on these portents and felt the blame like an illness in his breast.

sentences.zh: 〈疚，病也。

translations.literal: 〈Jiu means illness.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Jiu means inner sickness or remorse.〉

sentences.zh: 詩曰：「憂心孔疚。」〉

translations.literal: The Poetry says: ‘Anxious in heart, greatly ill.’〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Mao poem uses the same word for gnawing care.〉

sentences.zh: 其勑公卿舉賢良、方正各一人；

translations.literal: Let the dukes and ministers each recommend one worthy and good man of honest character;
translations.idiomatic: He commanded the high ministers each to nominate one worthy, upright candidate;

sentences.zh: 〈武帝建元元年，始詔舉賢良方正、直言極諫之士也。〉

translations.literal: 〈In the first year of Jianyuan Emperor Wu first ordered such recruitment of worthy, upright, and bluntly remonstrating scholars.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The practice dated to Emperor Wu’s Jianyuan edicts on recruitment.〉

sentences.zh: 百僚並上封事，無有隱諱；

translations.literal: let all officials jointly submit sealed memorials and hide nothing;
translations.idiomatic: all ranks were to submit sealed critiques without holding back;

sentences.zh: 〈宣帝始令羣臣得奏封事，以知下情。〉

translations.literal: 〈Emperor Xuan first allowed ministers to memorialize under seal so he could learn conditions below.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Sealed memorials had begun under Emperor Xuan to bypass court factions.〉

sentences.zh: 有司修職，務遵法度。」

translations.literal: and let those in charge restore their duties and strictly observe the statutes.”
translations.idiomatic: and responsible bureaus were to resume regular business within the law.”

sentences.zh: 十一月丁卯，詔王莽時吏人沒入為奴婢不應舊法者，皆免為庶人。

translations.literal: On dingmao in the eleventh month an edict ordered that clerks and commoners enslaved during Wang Mang’s time who did not match the old statutes should all be freed as commoners.
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao in the eleventh month everyone reduced to slavery under Wang Mang contrary to Former Han law was manumitted to commoner status.

sentences.zh: 十二月壬辰，大司空宋弘免。

translations.literal: On renchen in the twelfth month Grand Minister of Works Song Hong was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Song Hong was removed from office as grand minister of works on renchen in the twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 癸巳，詔曰：「頃者師旅未解，用度不足，故行十一之稅。

translations.literal: On guisi an edict said: “Recently, while the armies were not yet disbanded, expenses were insufficient, so an eleventh-part tax was levied.
translations.idiomatic: On guisi an edict explained: “While campaigns still drained the treasury, the court had levied a one-in-ten surtax.

sentences.zh: 〈謂十分而稅其一也。

translations.literal: 〈Meaning that one tenth was taken as tax.
translations.idiomatic: 〉That is, ten percent of the harvest.〉

sentences.zh: 孟子曰：「夏五十而貢，殷七十而助，周百畒而徹，其實皆什一也。」〉

translations.literal: 〉Mencius says: ‘Xia took one fiftieth by tribute, Yin one seventh by aid, Zhou one tenth by che; in fact all were a tenth.’〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Mencius summarizes the classical tithe systems to show the one-tenth norm.〉

sentences.zh: 今軍士屯田，糧儲差積。

translations.literal: Now the garrison soldiers till the fields and grain reserves have gradually accumulated.
translations.idiomatic: With soldiers farming garrison lands, granaries were slowly refilling.

sentences.zh: 〈武帝初通西域，始置校尉屯田。〉

translations.literal: 〈When Emperor Wu first opened the Western Regions he began to station colonels who farmed garrison fields.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The policy echoed Emperor Wu’s frontier agricultural colonies.〉

sentences.zh: 其令郡國收見田租三十稅一，如舊制。」

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that commanderies and kingdoms collect the field tax at one thirtieth, according to the old system.”
translations.idiomatic: He therefore restored the thirty-to-one land tax used before the emergency.”

sentences.zh: 〈景帝二年，令人田租三十而稅一，今依景帝，故云「舊制」。〉

translations.literal: 〈In the second year of Emperor Jing people were taxed one thirtieth of the field; now he follows Jing, hence ‘old system.’〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The ‘old system’ is Emperor Jing’s thirty-to-one rate, not Wang Mang’s exactions.〉

sentences.zh: 隗嚻遣將行巡寇扶風，

translations.literal: Wei Xiao sent the general Xing Xun to raid You Fufeng,
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao’s general Xing Xun (surname Xing) struck toward You Fufeng,

sentences.zh: 〈行，姓；

translations.literal: 〈Here the first graph is the uncommon surname Xing (same writing as “to walk”).
translations.idiomatic: 〉The surname is the rare Xing written with the “walk” graph, not the common homophones.〉

sentences.zh: 巡，名。

translations.literal: Xun is the given name.
translations.idiomatic: His given name was Xun.

sentences.zh: 漢有行祐，為趙相，見風俗通。〉

translations.literal: 〉The Han had Xing You, who became chancellor of Zhao, recorded in the Fengsu tong.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉An earlier Han official Xing You illustrates the rare surname.〉

sentences.zh: 征西大將軍馮異拒破之。

translations.literal: Grand general of the west Feng Yi met and defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi, grand general of the west, intercepted and shattered Xing Xun’s column.

sentences.zh: 是歲，初罷郡國都尉官。

translations.literal: That year the commandery and kingdom commandant posts were abolished for the first time.
translations.idiomatic: For the first time the court abolished the ubiquitous commandery commandants.

sentences.zh: 始遣列侯就國。

translations.literal: For the first time enfeoffed marquises were sent to their fiefs.
translations.idiomatic: Marquises were for the first time required to reside on their allotted estates.

sentences.zh: 匈奴遣使來獻，使中郎將報命。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu sent envoys with tribute, and a leader of court gentlemen was dispatched to return the courtesy.
translations.idiomatic: Northern envoys arrived with gifts, and a gentleman leader of the household was sent north to answer them.

sentences.zh: 〈漢官儀曰：「使匈奴中郎將，擁節，秩比二千石。

translations.literal: 〈The Han guan yi says: ‘The leader of court gentlemen for the Xiongnu carried credentials, rank equivalent to two thousand shi.’
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Xiongnu envoy’s Han counterpart held high rank and imperial credentials.〉

sentences.zh: 」匈奴傳云：「令中郎將韓統報命，賂遺金幣。」〉

translations.literal: 〉The Xiongnu zhuan says: ‘The leader of court gentlemen Han Tong returned the mission and presented gold and silk.’〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Later Han account adds that Han Tong carried rich gifts on the return embassy.〉

sentences.zh: 七年春正月丙申，詔中都官、三輔、郡、國出繫囚，非犯殊死，皆一切勿案其罪。

translations.literal: In the seventh year, on bingshen in the first month of spring, an edict ordered the capital offices, the three Fu, commanderies, and kingdoms to release all prisoners in custody who were not guilty of capital crimes and for all cases to waive prosecution.
translations.idiomatic: Early in the seventh year, on bingshen, a general jail delivery freed everyone not sentenced to death throughout the capital region and the provinces.

sentences.zh: 見徒免為庶民。

translations.literal: Convicts already serving were freed as commoners.
translations.idiomatic: Those already under sentence were released to commoner status.

sentences.zh: 耐罪亡命，吏以文除之。

translations.literal: Those guilty of ‘nai’ crimes who had fled and renounced their names were to be cleared by clerks on documentary authority.
translations.idiomatic: Men liable to the lighter ‘nai’ mutilation penalty who had fled and abandoned their identity papers could be cleared when officials drew up formal registers.

sentences.zh: 〈耐，輕刑之名。

translations.literal: 〈Nai is the label for a lighter mutilating sentence.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Nai denoted the shaven-brand class of punishments below hard labor.〉

sentences.zh: 前書音義曰：「一歲刑為罰作，二歲刑已上為耐。

translations.literal: The Yin Yi on the Former Han says: ‘One year’s sentence is called fa zuo; two years or more is called nai.’
translations.idiomatic: 〉Former Han jurisprudence distinguished one-year ‘fa zuo’ labor from two-year ‘nai’ sentences.〉

sentences.zh: 」耐音乃代反。

translations.literal: 〉Nai is pronounced nai dai (fanqie).〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The reading nai–dai is given by fanqie.〉

sentences.zh: 亡命謂犯耐罪而背名逃者。

translations.literal: “Fleeing life” means those who committed nai-level crimes and fled under false names.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The term covers fugitives from nai sentences living under assumed names.〉

sentences.zh: 令吏為文簿，記其姓名而除其罪，恐遂逃不歸，因失名籍。〉

translations.literal: Let clerks draw up registers of their names and remove their guilt, lest if they still flee and fail to return their names be lost from the rolls.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Officials were to record amnestied fugitives so they would not remain permanently outside the tax registers.”〉

sentences.zh: 又詔曰：「世以厚葬為德，薄終為鄙，至于富者奢僭，貧者單財，

translations.literal: Another edict said: “The age esteems lavish burial as virtue and slight funeral arrangements as shame, so that the rich exceed what is proper and the poor exhaust their property,
translations.idiomatic: A second edict attacked the fashion for extravagant tombs: the wealthy broke sumptuary rules while the poor bankrupted their families,

sentences.zh: 〈單，盡也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Dan means 'to exhaust' or 'utterly.'〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Here the gloss explains dan (in the phrase dan cai) as 'to use up entirely.'〉

sentences.zh: 法令不能禁，禮義不能止，倉卒乃知其咎。

translations.literal: Statutes cannot forbid it, ritual and rightness cannot halt it; only in sudden crisis does one grasp the harm.
translations.idiomatic: Neither law nor ceremony could curb the fashion for sumptuous tombs; families grasped the evil only when war and chaos stripped the mounds bare.

sentences.zh: 〈倉卒謂喪亂也。

translations.literal: 〈Cang cu means the disorder of bereavement and turmoil.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The commentator takes cang cu as the shock of mutiny and collapse, when tombs were opened wholesale.〉

sentences.zh: 諸厚葬者皆被發掘，故乃知其咎。

translations.literal: All who had buried lavishly had been exhumed; therefore then they knew the fault.
translations.idiomatic: Those who had heaped treasure in the earth saw their ancestors dug up by looters, and the lesson became painfully clear.

sentences.zh: 咎，惡也。〉

translations.literal: Jiu means evil.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Jiu in this sense is 'calamity' or 'moral fault,' not mere misfortune.〉

sentences.zh: 其布告天下，令知忠臣、孝子、慈兄、悌弟薄葬送終之義。」

translations.literal: Let it be proclaimed throughout the realm so that loyal ministers, filial sons, kind elder brothers, and respectful younger brothers may know the rightness of modest burial and sending off the dead.”
translations.idiomatic: An edict went out urging the empire to honor kin with spare funerals worthy of the virtuous labels the classics praise.”

sentences.zh: 二月辛巳，罷護漕都尉官。

translations.literal: On xinsi in the second month the office of colonel director of grain transport was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: The redundant post of protector-colonel for canal transport was struck in the second month on xinsi.

sentences.zh: 三月丁酉，詔曰：「今國有衆軍，並多精勇，冝且罷輕車、騎士、材官、樓船士及軍假吏，

translations.literal: On dingyou in the third month an edict said: “The state now keeps many hosts, all picked and brave; it is fitting for the time being to dismiss the light chariot men, mounted knights, foresters, tower-ship marines, and acting clerks of the armies,
translations.idiomatic: On dingyou in the third month Guangwu ordered a demobilization: standing forces were ample, so conscript categories—light charioteers, horse archers, crossbow specialists, riverine marines, and temporary army scribes—

sentences.zh: 〈漢官儀曰：「高祖命天下郡國選能引關蹶張，材力武猛者，以為輕車、騎士、材官、樓船，常以立秋後講肄課試，各有員數。

translations.literal: 〈The Han guan yi says: ‘The High Ancestor ordered every commandery and kingdom to select men who could draw the strong bow and kick the crossbow, men of martial mettle, to serve as light charioteers, mounted knights, foresters, and tower-ship crews; after Beginning of Autumn each year they drilled and were examined, each with fixed quotas.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The note lists Former Han categories of local militia raised by annual drill.〉

sentences.zh: 平地用車騎，山阻用材官，水泉用樓船。

translations.literal: On level ground chariots and cavalry were used; in mountain barriers foresters; on rivers tower ships.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Flat country favored chariotry, hills favored foot archers, and rivers favored war barges.〉

sentences.zh: 」軍假吏謂軍中權置吏也。

translations.literal: ’Acting clerks of the armies’ means clerks provisionally appointed within the camps.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Jun jia li were ad hoc staff attached to campaigns rather than regular county appointees.〉

sentences.zh: 今悉罷之。〉

translations.literal: Now all are dismissed.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Every one of those emergency grades was cut.〉

sentences.zh: 令還復民伍。」

translations.literal: Let them return to the commoners’ five-neighbor squads.”
translations.idiomatic: Demobilized men were folded back into the rural mutual-responsibility units.”

sentences.zh: 公孫述立隗嚻為朔寧王。

translations.literal: Gongsun Shu installed Wei Xiao as king of Shuoning.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Shu, playing kingmaker in the northwest, enfeoffed Wei Xiao as king of Shuoning.

sentences.zh: 癸亥晦，日有食之，避正殿，寑兵，不聽事五日。

translations.literal: On guihai, the last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse; he avoided the main hall, rested the weapons, and did not hear cases for five days.
translations.idiomatic: The month closed with an eclipse; the court drew the curtains on routine business, stood down the guard, and suspended audiences for five days.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：「吾德薄致災，讁見日月，

translations.literal: An edict said: “My virtue is slight and has brought calamity; reproof appears in the sun and moon,
translations.idiomatic: He confessed: “Thin virtue has drawn heaven’s rebuke; the luminaries themselves proclaim blame,”

sentences.zh: 〈讁，責也。

translations.literal: 〈Zhe means reproof or blame (the graph written with the “speech” element variant).
translations.idiomatic: 〉Zhe in this context is moral blame read from celestial omens.〉

sentences.zh: 音直革反。

translations.literal: Pronounced zhi ge (fanqie).
translations.idiomatic: 〉The gloss gives the fanqie reading zhi–ge.〉

sentences.zh: 左傳曰：「人君為政不用善，自取讁於日月之災也。」〉

translations.literal: The Zuozhuan says: ‘When a lord practices government without goodness, he brings reproof on himself from the disasters of sun and moon.’〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The canonical parallel warns that eclipses answer misrule.〉

sentences.zh: 戰慄恐懼，夫何言哉！

translations.literal: I tremble in fear; what words can I say!
translations.idiomatic: He added that he shook with dread and could find no excuse in words.

sentences.zh: 今方念愆，庶消厥咎。

translations.literal: Now as I ponder my faults, I hope thereby to dispel this blame.
translations.idiomatic: Only by owning fault and reforming administration could he hope to lift the omen.

sentences.zh: 其令有司各修職任，奉遵法度，惠茲元元。

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that the responsible offices each restore their duties, uphold the statutes, and show kindness to the common people.
translations.idiomatic: Bureaus were told to resume lawful routine and bring real relief to ordinary households.

sentences.zh: 百僚各上封事，無有所諱。

translations.literal: Let all officials submit sealed memorials and hide nothing;
translations.idiomatic: Every rank was to memorialize under seal with blunt candor;

sentences.zh: 其上書者，不得言聖。」

translations.literal: Those who submit memorials must not use the word ‘sage.’”
translations.idiomatic: petitioners were forbidden empty flattery that styled him a sage.”

sentences.zh: 夏四月壬午，詔曰：「比陰陽錯謬，日月薄食。

translations.literal: On renwu in the fourth month of summer an edict said: “Recently yin and yang have been disordered and sun and moon have narrowly eclipsed each other.
translations.idiomatic: That summer on renwu he cited disturbed cosmology—near misses of eclipse—as the pretext for mercy.

sentences.zh: 百姓有過，在予一人，大赦天下。

translations.literal: If the hundred surnames have fault, it lies in me alone; let there be a great amnesty for the realm.
translations.idiomatic: He took blame on himself and proclaimed a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 公、卿、司隷、州牧舉賢良、方正各一人，遣詣公車，朕將覽試焉。」

translations.literal: Let dukes, ministers, the colonel director of retainers, and provincial governors each recommend one worthy man of honest character and dispatch him to the public coach gate; I shall review and examine them.”
translations.idiomatic: High ministers and regional heads were each to forward one worthy, upright candidate to the public coach office for imperial interview.”

sentences.zh: 〈公車，門名。

translations.literal: 〈Gong che is a gate name.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Gong che denoted the palace gate where memorials arrived.〉

sentences.zh: 公車所在，因以名焉。

translations.literal: Where the public coaches stood, it took its name from that.
translations.idiomatic: The office took its label from the chariots that once waited there.

sentences.zh: 漢官儀曰：「公車掌殿司馬門，天下上事及徵召皆緫領之。」〉

translations.literal: The Han guan yi says: ‘The public coach office managed the Sima gate of the hall; all submissions from the realm and all summonses were centrally handled.’〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Its stewards screened paperwork and candidates for the throne.〉

sentences.zh: 五月戊戌，前將軍李通為大司空。

translations.literal: On wuxu in the fifth month former general Li Tong became grand minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: Li Tong, lately general of the van, was promoted to grand minister of works on wuxu.

sentences.zh: 甲寅，詔吏人遭饑亂及為青、徐賊所略為奴婢下妻，欲去留者，恣聽之。

translations.literal: On jiayin an edict said: As for clerks and commoners who met famine and rebellion or were carried off by bandits of Qing and Xu to become slave concubines or secondary wives, whether they wish to leave or stay, let them follow their own wish.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin the throne freed victims of Qing–Xu raiders: anyone enslaved during the famines could stay or go as they chose.

sentences.zh: 〈杜預注左傳云：「不以道取為略。」〉

translations.literal: 〈Du Yu’s note on the Zuozhuan says: ‘Taking by unjust means is lue.’〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Lue means seizure without right, the commentator stresses.〉

sentences.zh: 敢拘制不還，以賣人法從事。

translations.literal: Whoever dares detain them and not return them shall be dealt with under the statutes on selling people.
translations.idiomatic: Masters who refused release would be prosecuted like traffickers in human beings.

sentences.zh: 〈言從賣人之事以結其罪。〉

translations.literal: 〈Meaning that they are convicted by analogy to the crime of selling people.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The penalty matched laws against the slave trade.〉

sentences.zh: 是夏，連雨水。

translations.literal: That summer rain fell without cease.
translations.idiomatic: Steady downpours soaked the heartland all summer.

sentences.zh: 漢忠將軍王常為橫野大將軍。

translations.literal: General Wang Chang styled Zhonghan became grand general of the horizontal field.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Chang, the loyal general, received the banner rank grand general of the horizontal field.

sentences.zh: 八月丁亥，封前河閒王邵為河閒王。

translations.literal: On dinghai in the eighth month Shao, the former king of Hejian, was enfeoffed as king of Hejian.
translations.idiomatic: The former Hejian royal Liu Shao had his title restored on dinghai in the eighth month.

sentences.zh: 隗嚻寇安定，征西大將軍馮異、征虜將軍祭遵擊却之。

translations.literal: Wei Xiao raided Anding; grand general of the west Feng Yi and general who captures the enemy Zhai Zun drove him back.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao struck Anding but was thrown back by Feng Yi and Zhai Zun.

sentences.zh: 冬，盧芳所置朔方太守田颯、

translations.literal: In winter, Tian Sa, whom Lu Fang had installed as grand administrator of Shuofang,
translations.idiomatic: That winter, Tian Sa, the Shuofang governor Lu Fang had named,

sentences.zh: 〈音立。〉

translations.literal: 〈The character is pronounced li.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The commentary supplies the reading li for Sa.〉

sentences.zh: 雲中太守喬扈各舉郡降。

translations.literal: and Qiao Hu, grand administrator of Yunzhong, each raised their commandery and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: together with Qiao Hu of Yunzhong, opened their seals and came over to Han.

sentences.zh: 是歲，省長水、射聲二校尉官。

translations.literal: That year the posts of colonels of the Changshui and She Sheng corps were abolished.
translations.idiomatic: The paired metropolitan guard colonelcies of Changshui and She Sheng were cut for economy.

sentences.zh: 〈前書音義曰：「長水，地名，胡騎所屯。

translations.literal: 〈The Yin Yi on the Former Han says: ‘Changshui is a place name where Hu cavalry were stationed.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Changshui housed frontier horse archers attached to the capital guard.〉

sentences.zh: 射聲謂工射者也，夜中聞聲則射之，因以為名。

translations.literal: She sheng refers to skilled archers who, hearing a sound in the night, shot toward it; hence the name.
translations.idiomatic: She sheng archers were night skirmishers who loosed at noise in the dark, which gave the unit its title.

sentences.zh: 」二校尉皆武帝置，今省之。〉

translations.literal: ’Both colonels were established by Emperor Wu; now they are abolished.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Emperor Wu had created both commands; Guangwu suppressed them again.〉

sentences.zh: 八年春正月，中郎將來歙襲略陽，

translations.literal: In the eighth year, in the first month of spring, leader of court gentlemen Lai Xi raided Lueyang,
translations.idiomatic: Early in the eighth year Lai Xi struck Lueyang,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬天水郡，故城在今秦州隴城縣西北。〉

translations.literal: 〈A county name in Tianshui commandery; its old walled town lay northwest of Longcheng county in modern Qinzhou.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Lueyang guarded the Long approach into Wei Xiao’s heartland.〉

sentences.zh: 殺隗嚻守將而據其城。

translations.literal: killed Wei Xiao’s garrison commander, and seized the city.
translations.idiomatic: He killed the defender and planted the Han banner on the walls.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，司隷校尉傅抗下獄死。

translations.literal: In the fourth lunar month of summer the colonel director of retainers Fu Kang was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month Fu Kang, the colonel director of retainers, was thrown into prison and died there.

sentences.zh: 隗嚻攻來歙，不能下。

translations.literal: Wei Xiao attacked Lai Xi but could not take him.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao threw his whole army against Lai Xi’s bridgehead without dislodging it.

sentences.zh: 閏月，帝自征嚻，河西太守竇融率五郡太守與車駕會高平。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month the emperor himself campaigned against Xiao; grand administrator of Hexi Dou Rong led the grand administrators of five commanderies to join the imperial carriage at Gaoping.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor marched west in the intercalary month while Dou Rong brought five Hexi commanderies to rendezvous at Gaoping.

sentences.zh: 〈五郡謂隴西、金城、天水、酒泉、張掖。

translations.literal: 〈The five commanderies are Longxi, Jincheng, Tianshui, Jiuquan, and Zhangye.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The allies were the Long corridor commanderies still loyal to Luoyang.〉

sentences.zh: 高平，縣名，屬安定，後改為平高今原州縣。〉

translations.literal: Gaoping is a county name in Anding; later it was changed to Pinggao, now a county in Yuanzhou.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Gaoping (later renamed) sat on the northern route toward Anding.〉

sentences.zh: 隴右潰，隗嚻奔西城，遣大司馬吳漢、征南大將軍岑彭圍之；

translations.literal: The Long west collapsed; Wei Xiao fled to Xicheng; the great marshal Wu Han and grand general of the south Cen Peng besieged him;
translations.idiomatic: The Long front broke; Wei Xiao bolted to Xicheng while Wu Han and Cen Peng invested him;

sentences.zh: 進幸上邽，

translations.literal: the emperor advanced to Shanggui,
translations.idiomatic: the sovereign pressed on to Shanggui,

sentences.zh: 〈上邽，縣名，屬隴西郡，故邽戎邑，今秦州縣。〉

translations.literal: 〈Shanggui is a county name in Longxi commandery, the old settlement of the Gui Rong; it is now a county in Qinzhou.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Shanggui was the critical county seat west of the Long defile.〉

sentences.zh: 不降，命虎牙大將軍蓋延、建威大將軍耿弇攻之。

translations.literal: which did not submit; he ordered grand general Gai Yan styled Huya and grand general who establishes might Geng Yan to attack it.
translations.idiomatic: When the city refused terms, he ordered Gai Yan and Geng Yan to storm it.

sentences.zh: 潁川盜賊寇沒屬縣，河東守守兵亦叛，京師騷動。

translations.literal: Bandits of Yingchuan raided and overran subordinate counties; the garrison troops of Hedong’s defenders also rebelled, and the capital region was thrown into alarm.
translations.idiomatic: While the emperor was far west, Yingchuan outlaws pillaged dependencies and Hedong mutinied, jolting Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 秋，大水。

translations.literal: In autumn there was great flooding.
translations.idiomatic: Autumn brought widespread inundation.

sentences.zh: 八月，帝自上邽晨夜東馳。

translations.literal: In the eighth month the emperor rode east day and night from Shanggui.
translations.idiomatic: He abandoned the siege train and raced east from Shanggui through day and night.

sentences.zh: 九月乙卯，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On yimao in the ninth month the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: By yimao in the ninth month he was back in the capital.

sentences.zh: 庚申，帝自征潁川盜賊，皆降。

translations.literal: On gengshen the emperor personally campaigned against the Yingchuan bandits; all surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: On gengshen he led a lightning strike on the Yingchuan gangs and received their capitulation.

sentences.zh: 安丘侯張步叛歸琅邪，

translations.literal: Marquis of Anqiu Zhang Bu rebelled and returned to Langye,
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu, marquis of Anqiu, broke faith and fled toward Langye,

sentences.zh: 〈安丘，縣，屬北海郡，今密州縣，有渠丘亭。〉

translations.literal: 〈Anqiu is a county in Beihai commandery; it is now a county in Mizhou and has the Qiuqu pavilion.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The town lay in coastal Beihai near the modern Shandong shore.〉

sentences.zh: 琅邪太守陳俊討獲之。

translations.literal: Grand administrator of Langye Chen Jun attacked, captured him.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Jun, Langye’s governor, ran him to earth.

sentences.zh: 戊寅，至自潁川。

translations.literal: On wuyin he returned from Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: He was back from the Yingchuan expedition on wuyin.

sentences.zh: 冬十月丙午，幸懷。

translations.literal: On bingwu in the tenth month of winter he visited Huai.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month he toured Huai on bingwu.

sentences.zh: 十一月乙丑，至自懷。

translations.literal: On yichou in the eleventh month he returned from Huai.
translations.idiomatic: He completed the Huai circuit and re-entered the capital on yichou.

sentences.zh: 公孫述遣兵救隗嚻，吳漢、蓋延等還軍長安。

translations.literal: Gongsun Shu sent troops to relieve Wei Xiao; Wu Han, Gai Yan, and others withdrew their armies to Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Shu’s relief column forced Wu Han and Gai Yan to lift the siege and fall back on Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 天水、隴西復反歸嚻。

translations.literal: Tianshui and Longxi again revolted and went over to Xiao.
translations.idiomatic: No sooner had the emperor left than Tianshui and Longxi reverted to Wei Xiao.

sentences.zh: 十二月，高句麗王遣使奉貢。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month the king of Gaogouli sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: The Gaogouli ruler dispatched a mission with gifts in the twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 是歲大水。

translations.literal: That year there was great flooding.
translations.idiomatic: The year ended under another round of catastrophic floods.

sentences.zh: 〈左傳曰：「平原出水為大水。」〉

translations.literal: The Zuozhuan says: ‘When the plain becomes water, that is great flooding.’〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The classic definition distinguishes sheet flooding from ordinary high water.〉

sentences.zh: 九年春正月，隗嚻病死，其將王元、周宗復立嚻子純為王。

translations.literal: In the ninth year, in the first month of spring, Wei Xiao died of illness; his generals Wang Yuan and Zhou Zong set up Xiao’s son Chun as king in his stead.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao’s death in the ninth year’s first month left his generals to crown his young son Wei Chun.

sentences.zh: 徙鴈門吏人於太原。

translations.literal: The clerks and commoners of Yanmen were moved to Taiyuan.
translations.idiomatic: Population from Yanmen was resettled into Taiyuan for security.

sentences.zh: 三月辛亥，初置青巾左校尉官。

translations.literal: On xinhai in the third month the office of colonel of the left with the green kerchief was first established.
translations.idiomatic: A new metropolitan post, colonel of the left with green headcloth, was created on xinhai.

sentences.zh: 公孫述遣將田戎、任滿據荊門。

translations.literal: Gongsun Shu sent the generals Tian Rong and Ren Man to hold Jingmen.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Shu planted Tian Rong and Ren Man on the Jingmen narrows of the Yangzi.

sentences.zh: 〈水經注曰：「江水東歷荊門、虎牙之閒。

translations.literal: The Shui jing zhu says: ‘The Yangzi flows east past the gap between Jingmen and Huya.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The river commentary describes the famous Yangzi gateway.〉

sentences.zh: 荊門山在南，上合下開，其狀似門，虎牙山在北，石壁色紅，閒有白文類牙，故以名也。

translations.literal: Jingmen hill lies on the south; it joins above and opens below, shaped like a gate; Huya hill lies on the north; its red cliffs bear white markings like teeth, hence the names.
translations.idiomatic: South stood Jingmen ridge like a portal; north stood Huya’s red bluff scored with white veins like fangs.

sentences.zh: 此二山，楚之西塞也。

translations.literal: These two hills were the western barrier of Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Together they formed ancient Chu’s western rampart.

sentences.zh: 」在今硤州夷陵縣東南。〉

translations.literal: ’They are southeast of Yiling county in modern Xia prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The site lies east of present Yiling in the Three Gorges region.〉

sentences.zh: 夏六月丙戌，幸緱氏，登轘轅。

translations.literal: On bingxu in the sixth month of summer he visited Gou shi and ascended the Huanyuan pass.
translations.idiomatic: That summer on bingxu he sacrificed travel to climb the Huanyuan road east of Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 〈緱氏縣有緱氏山，轘轅山有轘轅阪，並在洛陽之東南。〉

translations.literal: 〈Gou shi county contains Mount Gou shi; Huanyuan mountain has the Huanyuan slope; both lie southeast of Luoyang.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The outing combined ritual ascent with inspection of the eastern approaches.〉

sentences.zh: 遣大司馬吳漢率四將軍擊盧芳將賈覽於高柳，戰不利。

translations.literal: He sent great marshal Wu Han leading four other generals to attack Lu Fang’s general Jia Lan at Gaoliu; the battle went badly.
translations.idiomatic: The northern thrust led by Wu Han against Jia Lan at Gaoliu stalled with heavy losses.

sentences.zh: 秋八月，遣中郎將來歙監征西大將軍馮異等五將軍討隗純於天水。

translations.literal: In the eighth month of autumn he sent leader of court gentlemen Lai Xi to supervise grand general of the west Feng Yi and five other generals in campaigning against Wei Chun in Tianshui.
translations.idiomatic: Lai Xi was given oversight of six generals—including Feng Yi—for the reduction of Wei Chun in Tianshui.

sentences.zh: 驃騎大將軍杜茂與賈覽戰於繁畤，

translations.literal: Grand general of agile cavalry Du Mao fought Jia Lan at Fanzhi,
translations.idiomatic: Du Mao, grand general of agile cavalry, met Jia Lan at Fanzhi,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬鴈門郡，今代州縣。〉

translations.literal: 〈A county name in Yanmen commandery; it is now a county in Daizhou.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Fanzhi lay in northern Yanmen near the steppe frontier.〉

sentences.zh: 茂軍敗績。

translations.literal: and Mao’s army was routed.
translations.idiomatic: and Du Mao’s line collapsed.

sentences.zh: 是歲，省關都尉，

translations.literal: That year the pass commandants were abolished,
translations.idiomatic: Frontier pass commandants were struck from the rolls,

sentences.zh: 〈前書曰秦官也，武帝置。〉

translations.literal: 〈The Former Han says they were a Qin office revived by Emperor Wu.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The post dated to Qin and Former Han frontier policy.〉

sentences.zh: 復置護羌校尉官。

translations.literal: and the colonel who protects the Qiang was re-established.
translations.idiomatic: while the old colonel who protects the Qiang was revived to handle western tribes.

sentences.zh: 〈漢官儀曰：「武帝置，秩比二千石，持節，以護西羌。

translations.literal: The Han guan yi says: ‘Emperor Wu established it, rank equivalent to two thousand shi, bearing credentials, to oversee the Qiang of the west.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The protector-colonel ranked with major governors and held imperial staff over western tribes.〉

sentences.zh: 王莽亂，遂罷。

translations.literal: Wang Mang’s turmoil caused it to be abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang had let the office lapse amid usurpation.

sentences.zh: 」時班彪議，冝復其官，以理冤結。

translations.literal: ’At this time Ban Biao argued that it should be restored to settle grievances.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Biao now urged the revival of the post to arbitrate tribal grievances.

sentences.zh: 帝從之，以牛邯為護羌校尉，都於隴西令居縣。〉

translations.literal: The emperor followed this, appointing Niu Han colonel who protects the Qiang, with headquarters in Lingju county of Longxi.〉
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed, naming Niu Han to the post and basing him at Lingju in Longxi.〉

sentences.zh: 十年春正月，大司馬吳漢率捕虜將軍王霸等五將軍擊賈覽於高柳，匈奴遣騎救覽，諸將與戰，郤之。

translations.literal: In the tenth year, in the first month of spring, great marshal Wu Han leading captive-catching general Wang Ba and four other generals attacked Jia Lan at Gaoliu; the Xiongnu sent cavalry to rescue Lan; the generals fought them and drove them back.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth year’s first month Wu Han and Wang Ba struck Jia Lan again; Xiongnu horse came to Jia Lan’s aid but were beaten off.

sentences.zh: 修理長安高廟。

translations.literal: The lofty temple to the High Ancestor at Chang’an was repaired.
translations.idiomatic: Work crews restored Gaozu’s Chang’an temple.

sentences.zh: 夏，征西大將軍馮異破公孫述將趙匡於天水，斬之。

translations.literal: In summer grand general of the west Feng Yi defeated Gongsun Shu’s general Zhao Kuang at Tianshui and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi smashed a general of Gongsun Shu, Zhao Kuang, at Tianshui and sent the head to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 征西大將軍馮異薨。

translations.literal: Grand general of the west Feng Yi died.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yi, grand general of the west, died soon after that victory.

sentences.zh: 秋八月己亥，幸長安，祠高廟，遂有事十一陵。

translations.literal: On jihai in the eighth month of autumn he visited Chang’an, sacrificed at the lofty temple, then performed the rites at the eleven imperial tombs.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn, on jihai in the eighth month, he reached Chang’an, offered at Gaozu’s temple, and completed sacrifices at the eleven Western Han tombs.

sentences.zh: 戊戌，進幸汧。

translations.literal: On wuxu he advanced and visited Qian.
translations.idiomatic: On wuxu he pressed on to Qian county in You Fufeng.

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬右扶風，故城在今隴州汧源縣。〉

translations.literal: 〈Qian is a county name in You Fufeng; its old walled town is in what is now Qianyuan county, Long prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The place lay in the Longshan foothills west of the capital.〉

sentences.zh: 隗嚻將高峻降。

translations.literal: Gao Jun, a general of Wei Xiao, surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao’s officer Gao Jun came over with his command.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，中郎將來歙等大破隗純於落門，

translations.literal: In the tenth month of winter leaders of court gentlemen including Lai Xi inflicted a great defeat on Wei Chun at Luomen,
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Lai Xi and allied generals shattered Wei Chun’s army at the Luomen defile,

sentences.zh: 〈前書曰天水兾縣有落門聚，在今渭州隴西縣東南；

translations.literal: 〈The Former Han says that Ji county in Tianshui had the Luomen settlement; it is southeast of Longxi county in modern Weizhou;
translations.idiomatic: 〉Luomen was a choke point on the road out of Tianshui toward the Wei valley.〉

sentences.zh: 有落門山，落門水出焉。〉

translations.literal: there is Mount Luomen, and the Luomen water issues from it.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉A mountain and stream there share the name.〉

sentences.zh: 其將王元奔蜀，純與周宗降，隴右平。

translations.literal: His general Wang Yuan fled to Shu; Chun with Zhou Zong surrendered, and the Long west was pacified.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Yuan escaped into Gongsun Shu’s domain while Wei Chun and Zhou Zong capitulated, clearing the Long corridor.

sentences.zh: 先零羌寇金城、隴西，

translations.literal: The Qiang of Xianling raided Jincheng and Longxi,
translations.idiomatic: Xianling Qiang tribes struck Jincheng and Longxi,

sentences.zh: 〈金城，郡，故城在今蘭州廣武縣之西南。〉

translations.literal: 〈Jincheng commandery; its old walled town lay southwest of Guangwu county in modern Lanzhou.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Jincheng guarded the upper Yellow River west of the metropolis.〉

sentences.zh: 來歙率諸將擊羌於五谿，大破之。

translations.literal: Lai Xi led the generals and attacked the Qiang at Wuxi, inflicting a great defeat.
translations.idiomatic: Lai Xi caught the raiders at Wuxi and broke their host with heavy loss.

sentences.zh: 〈續漢志曰隴西襄武縣有五谿聚。〉

translations.literal: 〈The Xu Han zhi says that Xiangwu county in Longxi had the Wuxi settlement.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The battle site lay in southern Longxi.〉

sentences.zh: 庚寅，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On gengyin the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: By gengyin the court was back in Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 是歲，省定襄郡，

translations.literal: That year Dingxiang commandery was abolished,
translations.idiomatic: The court struck Dingxiang commandery from the map,

sentences.zh: 〈定襄故城在今勝州界。〉

translations.literal: 〈The old site of Dingxiang lies within the borders of modern Sheng prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Its seat had lain on the northern loop toward the steppe.〉

sentences.zh: 徙其民於西河。

translations.literal: and its people were moved to Xihe.
translations.idiomatic: and the inhabitants were resettled into Xihe.

sentences.zh: 〈郡名，今石州離石縣。〉

translations.literal: 〈Xihe is a commandery name; it is now Lishi county in Shi prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Xihe centered on the Fen valley in modern Shanxi.〉

sentences.zh: 泗水王歙薨。

translations.literal: King She of Sishui died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu She, king of Sishui, died.

sentences.zh: 淄川王終薨。

translations.literal: King Zhong of Zichuan died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Zhong, king of Zichuan, died.

sentences.zh: 十一年春二月己卯，詔曰：「天地之性人為貴。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year, on jimao in the second month of spring, an edict said: “In the nature of heaven and earth man is the noblest.
translations.idiomatic: Early in the eleventh year, on jimao, an edict opened: “Among creatures of heaven and earth, human life is supreme.

sentences.zh: 其殺奴婢，不得減罪。」

translations.literal: Whoever kills a slave or maidservant shall not have the sentence mitigated.”
translations.idiomatic: Murder of a bondservant will henceforth receive no reduction in penalty.”

sentences.zh: 己酉，幸南陽；

translations.literal: On jiyou he visited Nanyang;
translations.idiomatic: On jiyou he toured Nanyang;

sentences.zh: 還，幸章陵，祠園陵。

translations.literal: returning, he visited Zhangling and sacrificed at the park tombs.
translations.idiomatic: on the return leg he stopped at Zhangling (his family seat) and offered at the ancestral graves.

sentences.zh: 城陽王祉薨。

translations.literal: King Zhi of Chengyang died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Zhi, king of Chengyang, died.

sentences.zh: 庚午，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On gengwu the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: He was home in the capital again on gengwu.

sentences.zh: 閏月，征南大將軍岑彭率三將軍與公孫述將田戎、任滿戰於荊門，大破之，獲任滿。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month grand general of the south Cen Peng leading three other generals fought Gongsun Shu’s generals Tian Rong and Ren Man at Jingmen, inflicted a great defeat, and captured Ren Man.
translations.idiomatic: In the intercalary month Cen Peng, with three generals, smashed Tian Rong and Ren Man at the Jingmen narrows and took Ren Man alive.

sentences.zh: 威虜將軍馮駿圍田戎於江州，

translations.literal: General who captures the enemy Feng Jun besieged Tian Rong at Jiangzhou,
translations.idiomatic: Feng Jun, general who captures the enemy, penned Tian Rong inside Jiangzhou,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬巴郡，今渝州巴縣。〉

translations.literal: 〈A county name in Ba commandery; it is now Ba county in Yu prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Jiangzhou corresponds to the Chongqing basin.〉

sentences.zh: 岑彭遂率舟師伐公孫述，平巴郡。

translations.literal: Cen Peng then led the river fleet to campaign against Gongsun Shu and pacified Ba commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Cen Peng descended the Yangzi with the war fleet, reduced Ba commandery, and opened the road into Shu.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丁卯，省大司徒司直官。

translations.literal: On dingmao in the fourth month of summer the office of rectifier under the grand minister of education was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao in the fourth month the redundant post of grand minister of education’s rectifier was cut.

sentences.zh: 〈漢官儀曰：「武帝置丞相司直，元壽二年改丞相為大司徒，司直仍舊。

translations.literal: 〈The Han guan yi says: “Emperor Wu established the rectifier under the chancellor; in the second year of Yuanshou the chancellor was renamed grand minister of education, but the rectifier remained.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉The office had policed ministers since Emperor Wu.〉

sentences.zh: 」今省。〉

translations.literal: ”Now it is abolished.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Guangwu ended it as part of streamlining.〉

sentences.zh: 先零羌寇臨洮。

translations.literal: The Qiang of Xianling raided Lintao.
translations.idiomatic: Xianling horsemen next struck Lintao on the western frontier.

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬隴西郡，故城在今岷州。〉

translations.literal: 〈Lintao is a county in Longxi commandery; its old walled town is in what is now Min prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Lintao guarded the Tao River approach toward the Tibetan plateau fringe.〉

sentences.zh: 六月，中郎將來歙率揚武將軍馬成破公孫述將王元、環安於下辯。

translations.literal: In the sixth month leader of court gentlemen Lai Xi leading general Ma Cheng styled Yangwu defeated Gongsun Shu’s generals Wang Yuan and Huan An at Xiabian.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Lai Xi and Ma Cheng broke Wang Yuan and Huan An at Xiabian in Wudu.

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬武都郡，今成州同谷縣，舊名武衞城。〉

translations.literal: 〈Xiabian is a county in Wudu commandery; it is now Tonggu county in Cheng prefecture, formerly called Weicheng.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The town sat on the Hanzhong–Long corridor.〉

sentences.zh: 安遣間人刺殺中郎將來歙。

translations.literal: An sent an agent to assassinate leader of court gentlemen Lai Xi.
translations.idiomatic: Huan An slipped an assassin through the lines who mortally wounded Lai Xi.

sentences.zh: 〈間，諜也，謂伺候閒隙也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Jian means spy; it means watching for an opening.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Jian here denotes a covert operative waiting for a gap in security.〉

sentences.zh: 帝自將征公孫述。

translations.literal: The emperor personally led a campaign against Gongsun Shu.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then took the field in person against Gongsun Shu.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，次長安。

translations.literal: In the seventh month of autumn he halted at Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn, in the seventh month, he staged at Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 〈左傳例曰：「凡師出一宿為舍，再宿為信，過信為次。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Li on the Zuozhuan says: “Whenever an army spends one night abroad it is called she, two nights xin, more than xin is called ci.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The classic gloss distinguishes overnight halts from a longer encampment (ci).〉

sentences.zh: 八月，岑彭破公孫述將侯丹於黃石。

translations.literal: In the eighth month Cen Peng defeated Gongsun Shu’s general Hou Dan at Huangshi.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Cen Peng crushed Hou Dan at the Huangshi rapids.

sentences.zh: 〈即黃石灘也。

translations.literal: 〈That is the Huangshi rapid.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Huangshi was a notorious shoal in the Yangzi gorges.〉

sentences.zh: 水經注曰：「江水自涪陵東出百里而屆于黃石。

translations.literal: The Shui jing zhu says: “The Yangzi east from Fuling goes a hundred li and reaches Huangshi.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The river commentary traces the reach east of Fuling.〉

sentences.zh: 」在今涪州涪陵縣。〉

translations.literal: ”It is in Fuling county of Fu prefecture today.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The site lies in modern Fuling.〉

sentences.zh: 輔威將軍臧宮與公孫述將延岑戰於沈水，大破之。

translations.literal: General who aids might Zang Gong fought Gongsun Shu’s general Yan Cen at the Shen River and inflicted a great defeat.
translations.idiomatic: Zang Gong met Yan Cen on the Shen River and routed him.

sentences.zh: 〈水經注曰：「沈水出廣漢縣，下入涪水。

translations.literal: The Shui jing zhu says: “The Shen River issues from Guanghan county and flows down into the Fu River.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Shen is a tributary entering the Fu above Jiangzhou.〉

sentences.zh: 」本或作「沉水」及「沅水」者，並非。〉

translations.literal: ”Manuscripts that write “sinking Shen” or “Yuan River” are both wrong.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Other graph variants for the stream name are rejected by the commentator.〉

sentences.zh: 王元降。

translations.literal: Wang Yuan surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Yuan, isolated, gave up the sword.

sentences.zh: 至自長安。

translations.literal: He arrived from Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then returned from the Chang’an expedition.

sentences.zh: 癸亥，詔曰：「敢灸灼奴婢，論如律，免所灸灼者為庶民。」

translations.literal: On guihai an edict said: “Whoever dares cauterize slaves or maidservants shall be sentenced according to statute, and those who were cauterized shall be freed as commoners.”
translations.idiomatic: On guihai branding or burning bondsmen without legal cause was criminalized and victims were manumitted.

sentences.zh: 冬十月壬午，詔除奴婢射傷人弃市律。

translations.literal: On renwu in the tenth month of winter an edict abolished the statute that bondmaids who shot and wounded people were executed in the market.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month on renwu he repealed the harsh rule that a slave who wounded someone with a bow must die in the public square.

sentences.zh: 公孫述遣間人刺殺征南大將軍岑彭。

translations.literal: Gongsun Shu sent an agent who assassinated grand general of the south Cen Peng.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Shu’s assassins caught Cen Peng in his camp and killed him.

sentences.zh: 馬成平武都，因隴西太守馬援擊破先零羗，徙致天水、隴西、扶風。

translations.literal: Ma Cheng pacified Wudu; relying on grand administrator of Longxi Ma Yuan he broke the Xianling Qiang and moved them to Tianshui, Longxi, and You Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Cheng secured Wudu while Ma Yuan drove the Xianling Qiang; survivors were deported into Tianshui, Longxi, and You Fufeng.

sentences.zh: 十二月，大司馬吳漢率舟師伐公孫述。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month great marshal Wu Han led the river fleet to campaign against Gongsun Shu.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Wu Han took the fleet downriver for the final push on Chengdu.

sentences.zh: 是歲，省朔方牧，并并州。

translations.literal: That year the shepherd of Shuofang was abolished and merged into Bingzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Provincial administration for Shuofang was folded into Bingzhou that year.

sentences.zh: 〈朔方，郡，在今夏州朔方縣北，上并音必政反。〉

translations.literal: 〈Shuofang commandery lies north of modern Xia prefecture in Shuofang county; the graph 并 in merge is read with the fanqie bi zheng.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The note marks pronunciation and locates the old commandery seat.〉

sentences.zh: 初斷州牧自還奏事。

translations.literal: For the first time the practice whereby provincial shepherds returned in person to report at court was cut off.
translations.idiomatic: Provincial governors were no longer allowed to travel to the capital each year for personal audience reports.

sentences.zh: 〈前書音義曰「刺史每歲盡則入奏事京師」，今斷之。

translations.literal: 〈The Yin Yi on the Former Han says: “Each year when finished the inspector entered the capital to report; now that is stopped.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉Former Han inspectors had annual memorial journeys; Guangwu ended the custom.〉

sentences.zh: 哀帝改刺史曰州牧。〉

translations.literal: Emperor Ai had changed the title inspector to shepherd.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The title “shepherd” dated from Emperor Ai’s renaming of the provincial inspector.〉

sentences.zh: 十二年春正月，大司馬吳漢與公孫述將史興戰於武陽，斬之。

translations.literal: In the twelfth year, in the first month of spring, great marshal Wu Han fought Gongsun Shu’s general Shi Xing at Wuyang and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth year’s first month Wu Han killed Shi Xing at Wuyang in Qianwei.

sentences.zh: 〈武陽，縣，屬犍為郡，故城在今眉州隆山縣東也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Wuyang is a county in Qianwei commandery; its old walled town is east of Longshan county in Mei prefecture today.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Wuyang lay on the Chengdu plain approach from the east.〉

sentences.zh: 三月癸酉，詔隴、蜀民被略為奴婢自訟者，及獄官未報，一切免為庶民。

translations.literal: On guiyou in the third month an edict said: As for people of Long and Shu who were carried off to become slaves or maidservants and who petition on their own, and for whom jail officials have not yet returned a verdict, all are to be freed as commoners.
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou a sweeping edict freed every Long or Shu captive still in servitude whose case had not been closed.

sentences.zh: 夏，甘露降南行唐。

translations.literal: In summer sweet dew fell on Nanxingtang.
translations.idiomatic: Sweet dew—an auspicious condensation—was reported at Nanxingtang that summer.

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬常山郡，今恒州縣。〉

translations.literal: 〈Nanxingtang is a county in Changshan commandery; it is now a county in Heng prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The omen occurred in the Zhao heartland south of the Yan range.〉

sentences.zh: 六月，黃龍見東阿。

translations.literal: In the sixth month a yellow dragon was seen in Dong’e.
translations.idiomatic: A yellow dragon sighting was recorded at Dong’e in the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 〈今濟州縣。〉

translations.literal: 〈It is now a county in Ji prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Dong’e lay in western Shandong.〉

sentences.zh: 秋七月，威虜將軍馮駿拔江州，獲田戎。

translations.literal: In the seventh month of autumn general who captures the enemy Feng Jun took Jiangzhou and captured Tian Rong.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Jun stormed Jiangzhou in the seventh month and seized Tian Rong.

sentences.zh: 九月，吳漢大破公孫述將謝豐于廣都，斬之。

translations.literal: In the ninth month Wu Han inflicted a great defeat on Gongsun Shu’s general Xie Feng at Guangdu and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Wu Han decapitated Xie Feng at Guangdu east of Chengdu.

sentences.zh: 〈廣都，今益州。〉

translations.literal: 〈Guangdu is in modern Yi prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Guangdu was the forward base on the plain before the capital.〉

sentences.zh: 輔威將軍臧宮拔涪城，斬公孫恢。

translations.literal: General who aids might Zang Gong took Fu city and beheaded Gongsun Hui.
translations.idiomatic: Zang Gong seized Fu and executed Gongsun Hui.

sentences.zh: 〈涪城，今綿州縣也。

translations.literal: 〈Fu city is now a county in Mian prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Fu guarded the Fu River line into the basin.〉

sentences.zh: 恢，述之弟。〉

translations.literal: Hui was Shu’s younger brother.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Hui was Gongsun Shu’s own brother.〉

sentences.zh: 大司空李通罷。

translations.literal: Grand minister of works Li Tong was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Li Tong vacated the office of grand minister of works.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月戊寅，吳漢、臧宮與公孫述戰於成都，大破之。

translations.literal: On wuyin in the eleventh month of winter Wu Han and Zang Gong fought Gongsun Shu at Chengdu and inflicted a great defeat.
translations.idiomatic: On wuyin in winter Wu Han and Zang Gong broke Gongsun Shu’s field army under the walls of Chengdu.

sentences.zh: 述被創，夜死。

translations.literal: Shu was wounded and died in the night.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Shu took a wound that night and died.

sentences.zh: 辛巳，吳漢屠成都，夷述宗族及延岑等。

translations.literal: On xinsi Wu Han slaughtered Chengdu and extirpated Shu’s clan and Yan Cen and others.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi Wu Han sacked Chengdu and extirpated the Gongsun lineage together with Yan Cen’s faction.

sentences.zh: 〈廣雅曰：「夷猶滅也。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Guangya says: “Yi means to wipe out.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Yi in annals usage means wholesale extermination of a house.〉

sentences.zh: 十二月辛卯，揚武將軍馬成行大司空事。

translations.literal: On xinmao in the twelfth month general Ma Cheng styled Yangwu acted in the affairs of grand minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: On xinmao Ma Cheng was placed in charge of the grand minister of works bureau ad interim.

sentences.zh: 十三年春正月庚申，大司徒侯霸薨。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year, on gengshen in the first month of spring, grand minister of education Hou Ba died.
translations.idiomatic: Hou Ba, grand minister of education, died early in the thirteenth year on gengshen.

sentences.zh: 戊子，詔曰：「往年已勑郡國，異味不得有所獻御，今猶未止，非徒有豫養導擇之勞，

translations.literal: On wuzi an edict said: “In past years I already ordered commanderies and kingdoms not to present exotic flavors to the palace, yet it has not yet stopped; not only is there the labor of rearing them in advance and selecting and guiding them,
translations.idiomatic: On wuzi he complained that despite prior bans, localities still forwarded rare delicacies, wasting effort on advance fattening and culling,

sentences.zh: 〈豫養謂未至獻時豫前養之。

translations.literal: 〈Yu yang means rearing them in advance before the time of presentation.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Yu yang meant keeping tribute beasts and foods on hand months before delivery.〉

sentences.zh: 導亦擇也。〉

translations.literal: dao also means selection.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Dao here carries the sense of careful picking for the table.〉

sentences.zh: 至乃煩擾道上，疲費過所。

translations.literal: so that they even vex those along the road and exhaust resources in every place they pass.
translations.idiomatic: and convoys harassed every relay and drained every county along the route.

sentences.zh: 其令太官勿復受。

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that the grand provisioner shall no longer accept them.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial kitchen was forbidden to accept such shipments.

sentences.zh: 〈續漢志曰：「太官令一人，秩六百石，掌御膳飲食。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Xu Han zhi says: “There is one grand provisioner, rank six hundred shi, who manages imperial meals and drink.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The grand provisioner’s warrant ran to all palace victualing.〉

sentences.zh: 明勑下以遠方口實所以薦宗廟，自如舊制。」

translations.literal: Let it be clearly ordered below that whatever distant local products are used to sacrifice in the ancestral temples shall follow the old system.”
translations.idiomatic: Temple offerings from afar might still follow standing ritual quotas.”

sentences.zh: 〈漢官儀曰：「口實，膳羞之事也。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Han guan yi says: “Kou shi means the business of viands.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Kou shi meant the substantive foods set before the shrines.〉

sentences.zh: 二月，遣捕虜將軍馬武屯虖沱河以備匈奴。

translations.literal: In the second month he sent general Ma Wu styled captive catcher to encamp on the Hutuo River to guard against the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month Ma Wu camped on the Hutuo line facing the steppe.

sentences.zh: 盧芳自五原亡入匈奴。

translations.literal: Lu Fang fled from Wuyuan into the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Fang abandoned Wuyuan and rode into Xiongnu protection.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，詔曰：「長沙王興、真定王得、河閒王邵、中山王茂，皆襲爵為王，不應經義。

translations.literal: On bingchen an edict said: “The kings Xing of Changsha, De of Zhending, Shao of Hejian, and Mao of Zhongshan all inherited titles as kings, which does not accord with canonical principle.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen he ruled that four kings who had merely inherited regional seats lacked classical warrant for full kingship,

sentences.zh: 〈以其服屬旣疏，不當襲爵為王。〉

translations.literal: 〈Because their degree of kinship was already distant, they ought not inherit the rank of king.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Their blood tie to the imperial house was too remote for true kingdoms.〉

sentences.zh: 其以興為臨湘侯，

translations.literal: let Xing be made marquis of Linxiang,
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xing was demoted to marquis of Linxiang,

sentences.zh: 〈臨湘，縣，今潭州長沙縣。〉

translations.literal: 〈Linxiang is a county; it is now Changsha county in Tan prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The new fief anchored him at the Chu capital district.〉

sentences.zh: 得為真定侯，邵為樂成侯，

translations.literal: De be made marquis of Zhending, Shao marquis of Lecheng,
translations.idiomatic: Liu De kept a Zhending marquisate, Liu Shao a Lecheng marquisate,

sentences.zh: 〈樂成，縣，故城在今瀛州樂府縣西北。〉

translations.literal: 〈Lecheng is a county; its old walled town is northwest of Yuefu county in Ying prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Lecheng lay in the Hejian region.〉

sentences.zh: 茂為單父侯。」

translations.literal: and Mao be made marquis of Shanfu.”
translations.idiomatic: and Liu Mao was given Shanfu in Song.”

sentences.zh: 〈今宋州縣。

translations.literal: 〈It is now a county in Song prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Shanfu sat on the Lu southwest of Qufu.〉

sentences.zh: 音善甫。〉

translations.literal: Pronounced shan fu.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The disyllable is read shan–fu.〉

sentences.zh: 其宗室及絕國封侯者凡一百三十七人。

translations.literal: In all, one hundred thirty-seven persons of the imperial clan and of extinguished states were enfeoffed as marquises.
translations.idiomatic: One hundred thirty-seven imperial clansmen and heirs of fallen houses received marquisates in the same restructuring.

sentences.zh: 丁巳，降趙王良為趙公，太原王章為齊公，魯王興為魯公。

translations.literal: On dingsi King Liang of Zhao was demoted to duke of Zhao, King Zhang of Taiyuan to duke of Qi, and King Xing of Lu to duke of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: On dingsi three kings—Liu Liang, Liu Zhang, and Liu Xing—were reduced to ducal rank while keeping their regional labels.

sentences.zh: 庚午，以殷紹嘉公孔安為宋公，周承休公姬常為衞公。

translations.literal: On gengwu Kong An, duke who continues Yin and Shaojia, was made duke of Song, and Ji Chang, duke who inherits Zhou and Xiuxiu, was made duke of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: On gengwu two ritual scions, Kong An and Ji Chang, received the revived titles duke of Song and duke of Wei.

sentences.zh: 省并西京十三國：廣平屬鉅鹿，真定屬常山，河閒屬信都，城陽屬琅邪，泗水屬廣陵，淄川屬高密，膠東屬北海，六安屬廬江，廣陽屬上谷。

translations.literal: Western capital thirteen kingdoms were abolished and merged: Guangping was attached to Julu, Zhending to Changshan, Hejian to Xindu, Chengyang to Langye, Sishui to Guangling, Zichuan to Gaomi, Jiaodong to Beihai, Lu’an to Lujiang, Guangyang to Shanggu.
translations.idiomatic: Nine former kingdoms around the old western capital were folded into larger commanderies—Guangping into Julu, Zhending into Changshan, Hejian into Xindu, Chengyang into Langye, Sishui into Guangling, Zichuan into Gaomi, Jiaodong into Beihai, Lu’an into Lujiang, and Guangyang into Shanggu.

sentences.zh: 〈據此惟有九國，云「十三」，誤也。〉

translations.literal: 〈According to this there are only nine kingdoms; saying “thirteen” is an error.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The text’s “thirteen” is a scribal error; only nine units were actually merged.〉

sentences.zh: 三月辛未，沛郡太守韓歆為大司徒。

translations.literal: In the third month, on xinwei, grand administrator of Pei Han Xin became grand minister of education.
translations.idiomatic: On xinwei in the third month Han Xin of Pei commandery took over as grand minister of education.

sentences.zh: 丙子，行大司空馬成罷。

translations.literal: On bingzi Ma Cheng, acting grand minister of works, was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Cheng stepped down from his interim grand minister of works post on bingzi.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，大司馬吳漢自蜀還京師，於是大饗將士，班勞策勳。

translations.literal: In the fourth month of summer great marshal Wu Han returned from Shu to the capital; thereupon a great feast was held for the generals and officers, and rewards were distributed and deeds of merit recorded in edicts.
translations.idiomatic: When Wu Han brought the army home that summer, the court feasted the host, handed out honors, and enfeoffed merit in formal edicts.

sentences.zh: 〈班，布也。

translations.literal: 〈Ban means to spread.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Ban here means to promulgate widely.〉

sentences.zh: 謂徧布勞來之。

translations.literal: It means to spread comfort and encouragement everywhere.
translations.idiomatic: 〉It describes comforting and encouraging the troops at large.〉

sentences.zh: 其有功者，以策書紀其勳也。

translations.literal: Those with merit had their deeds written up in document edicts.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Meritorious officers received written citations in the ce-book style.〉

sentences.zh: 勞音力到反。〉

translations.literal: Lao is pronounced with the fanqie li dao.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The gloss gives the reading lao as li–dao.〉

sentences.zh: 功臣增邑更封，凡三百六十五人。

translations.literal: Meritorious subjects had their fiefs enlarged or were re-enfeoffed; in all three hundred sixty-five persons.
translations.idiomatic: Three hundred sixty-five meritorious officials received enlarged or new fiefs.

sentences.zh: 其外戚恩澤封者四十五人。

translations.literal: Forty-five persons were enfeoffed through grace to the imperial in-laws.
translations.idiomatic: Another forty-five seals went to affines by special grace.

sentences.zh: 罷左右將軍官。

translations.literal: The offices of left and right generals were abolished.
translations.idiomatic: The paired posts of left and right generals were struck.

sentences.zh: 〈前書曰左右將軍，周官也，秦、漢因之。

translations.literal: 〈The Former Han says that left and right generals were Zhou offices continued by Qin and Han.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Those commands dated to Zhou military organization.〉

sentences.zh: 至此罷。〉

translations.literal: Here they are abolished.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Guangwu ended them as redundant.〉

sentences.zh: 建威大將軍耿弇罷。

translations.literal: Grand general who establishes might Geng Yan was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Yan vacated the title grand general who establishes might.

sentences.zh: 益州傳送公孫述瞽師、郊廟樂器、葆車、輿輦，於是法物始備。
