translations.literal: Yi commandery forwarded Gongsun Shu’s blind musicians, suburban and temple vessels, feather-canopied chariots, and palanquins; thereby the ritual paraphernalia were complete for the first time.
translations.idiomatic: Transports from Yi brought Shu’s blind orchestra, suburban and ancestral regalia, canopied state cars, and litters—so Luoyang finally possessed a full imperial train.

sentences.zh: 〈瞽，無目之人也。

translations.literal: 〈Gu means persons without eyes.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Gu musicians were blind men trained for the bell orchestra.〉

sentences.zh: 為樂師，取其無所見，於音聲審也。

translations.literal: As music masters they were chosen because, seeing nothing, they are acute in sound.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Blindness was thought to sharpen hearing for pitch.〉

sentences.zh: 郊廟之器，樽彝之屬也。

translations.literal: Vessels for suburban and temple rites are the class of zun and yi.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Bronze zun and yi sacrificial sets are meant.〉

sentences.zh: 樂器，鍾磬之屬。

translations.literal: Musical instruments are the class of bells and chimes.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Bells, lithophones, and drums filled out the orchestra.〉

sentences.zh: 葆車謂上建羽葆也。

translations.literal: A bao chariot means one on which a feather canopy is raised.
translations.idiomatic: 〉State coaches bore the five-colored feather bao canopy.〉

sentences.zh: 合聚五釆羽名為葆。

translations.literal: Gathering five-colored feathers together is called bao.
translations.idiomatic: 〉“Bao” named the clustered plumes atop the pole.〉

sentences.zh: 輿者，車之緫名也。

translations.literal: Yu is the general term for chariots.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Yu covered ordinary riding vehicles.〉

sentences.zh: 輦者，駕人以行。

translations.literal: Nian means drawn by men on foot.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Imperial litters were man-borne sedan chairs.〉

sentences.zh: 法物謂大駕鹵簿儀式也。

translations.literal: Fa wu means the great chariot escort and ceremonial order.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The phrase denotes the full outer and inner ritual guard.〉

sentences.zh: 時草創未暇，今得之始備。〉

translations.literal: At the time things were hastily begun and there had been no leisure; now that these were obtained they were complete for the first time.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The court had lacked regalia since the founding scramble; Shu’s hoard made the set whole.〉

sentences.zh: 時兵革旣息，天下少事，文書調役，

translations.literal: At this time arms were already laid down and the realm had few affairs; documents and levies in labor
translations.idiomatic: With war ended, paperwork and corvée calls

sentences.zh: 〈調謂發也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Diao means to issue.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Diao meant dispatching labor or grain requisitions.〉

sentences.zh: 務從簡寡，至乃十存一焉。

translations.literal: were required to follow simplicity and reduction, so that in the end only one in ten of the former remained.
translations.idiomatic: were pared back until roughly one-tenth of the old Han volume remained.

sentences.zh: 甲寅，兾州牧竇融為大司空。

translations.literal: On jiayin provincial shepherd Dou Rong of Ji became grand minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong moved from Ji shepherd to grand minister of works on jiayin.

sentences.zh: 五月，匈奴寇河東。

translations.literal: In the fifth month the Xiongnu raided Hedong.
translations.idiomatic: Hedong suffered a Xiongnu raid that fifth month.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，廣漢徼外白馬羌豪率種人內屬。

translations.literal: In the seventh month of autumn beyond the barrier of Guanghan chiefs of the White Horse Qiang led their clansmen to submit as subjects.
translations.idiomatic: White Horse Qiang beyond Guanghan’s frontier fence came in under their headmen that autumn.

sentences.zh: 〈廣漢，今益州雒縣也。

translations.literal: 〈Guanghan is modern Luo county in Yi prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The commandery seat was at modern Guanghan north of Chengdu.〉

sentences.zh: 徼猶塞也，音吉弔反。

translations.literal: Jiao means barrier; it is pronounced ji diao (fanqie).
translations.idiomatic: 〉Jiao meant the mountain barrier line; the reading is ji–diao.〉

sentences.zh: 羌有百五十四種，在廣漢西北者為白馬羌。〉

translations.literal: There are one hundred fifty-four Qiang tribes; those northwest of Guanghan are the White Horse Qiang.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The commentary counts Qiang branches and locates the White Horse west of the basin.〉

sentences.zh: 九月，日南徼外蠻夷獻白雉、白兔。

translations.literal: In the ninth month beyond the barrier of Rinan barbarians of the south presented white pheasants and white hares.
translations.idiomatic: Rinan frontier peoples sent white pheasants and hares as tribute in the ninth month.

sentences.zh: 〈日南，郡，屬交州。〉

translations.literal: 〈Rinan is a commandery belonging to Jiao province.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Rinan lay on the central Vietnam coast.〉

sentences.zh: 冬十二月甲寅，詔益州民自八年以來被略為奴婢者，

translations.literal: On jiayin in the twelfth month of winter an edict said: As for the people of Yi province who since the eighth year have been carried off to become slaves or maidservants,
translations.idiomatic: A winter edict on jiayin targeted Shu bondage: anyone enslaved since the eighth year,

sentences.zh: 〈謂公孫述時也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Meaning the time of Gongsun Shu.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉that is, during Gongsun Shu’s regime,〉

sentences.zh: 皆一切免為庶民；

translations.literal: all without exception are freed as commoners;
translations.idiomatic: was freed outright to commoner status;

sentences.zh: 或依託為人下妻，欲去者，恣聽之；

translations.literal: or if they have relied on registration as someone’s secondary wife and wish to leave, let them follow their own wish;
translations.idiomatic: women kept as concubines could leave or stay at will;

sentences.zh: 敢拘留者，比青、徐二州以略人法從事。

translations.literal: whoever dares detain them shall be dealt with under the statutes on carrying off people like those of Qing and Xu.
translations.idiomatic: detainers faced the same penalties as Qing–Xu slave raiders.

sentences.zh: 復置金城郡。

translations.literal: Jincheng commandery was re-established.
translations.idiomatic: Jincheng commandery was revived on the map.

sentences.zh: 〈前年省并隴西。〉

translations.literal: 〈The year before it had been merged into Longxi.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉It had been absorbed into Longxi the previous year.〉

sentences.zh: 十四年春正月，起南宮前殿。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year, in the first month of spring, the front hall of the southern palace was begun.
translations.idiomatic: Construction started on the southern palace’s front hall in the first month of the fourteenth year.

sentences.zh: 匈奴遣使奉獻，使中郎將報命。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu sent envoys with tribute, and a leader of court gentlemen was dispatched to return the courtesy.
translations.idiomatic: Xiongnu envoys brought gifts; a gentleman leader answered the mission.

sentences.zh: 〈中郎將劉襄也。〉

translations.literal: 〈The leader of court gentlemen was Liu Xiang.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The envoy named was Liu Xiang.〉

sentences.zh: 夏四月辛巳，封孔子後志為襃成侯。

translations.literal: On xinsi in the fourth month of summer a descendant of Confucius, Zhi, was enfeoffed as marquis of Bao cheng.
translations.idiomatic: Kong Zhi, Confucius’s heir, received the revived marquisate of Bao cheng on xinsi.

sentences.zh: 〈平帝封孔均為褎成侯。

translations.literal: 〈Emperor Ping had enfeoffed Kong Jun as marquis of Bao cheng.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The title went back to Emperor Ping’s grant to Kong Jun.〉

sentences.zh: 志，均子也。

translations.literal: Zhi was Jun’s son.
translations.idiomatic: Kong Zhi was Kong Jun’s son.

sentences.zh: 古今志曰志時為密令。〉

translations.literal: The Gu jin zhi says that at the time Zhi was magistrate of Mi.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉A note adds that Zhi was then serving as magistrate of Mi.〉

sentences.zh: 越巂人任貴自稱太守，遣使奉計。

translations.literal: A man of Yuexi, Ren Gui, styled himself grand administrator and sent envoys with registers.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Gui of Yuexi declared himself governor and forwarded household registers to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 〈越巂，郡，武帝置，本邛都也。

translations.literal: 〈Yuexi is a commandery established by Emperor Wu; originally it was Qiongdu.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The commandery lay on the Yi tribal frontier southwest of Shu.〉

sentences.zh: 巂，水名，因越巂水而置郡，故以名焉。

translations.literal: Xi is a river name; because they crossed the Yuexi river the commandery was established, hence it takes its name.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The name came from the Yuexi River crossing.〉

sentences.zh: 計謂人庶名籍，若今計帳。〉

translations.literal: Ji means the registers of people and households, like modern tax ledgers.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Ji denoted census-style account books.〉

sentences.zh: 秋九月，平城人賈丹殺盧芳將尹由來降。

translations.literal: In the ninth month of autumn Jia Dan of Pingcheng killed Lu Fang’s general Yin You and came over in surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Jia Dan murdered Lu Fang’s officer Yin You at Pingcheng and defected.

sentences.zh: 〈平城屬鴈門郡，今雲州定襄縣也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Pingcheng belonged to Yanmen commandery; it is now Dingxiang county in Yun prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Pingcheng guarded the Dai basin approach.〉

sentences.zh: 是歲，會稽大疫。

translations.literal: That year there was a great epidemic in Kuaiji.
translations.idiomatic: Kuaiji suffered a severe plague that year.

sentences.zh: 〈會稽，今越州縣。〉

translations.literal: 〈Kuaiji is now a county in Yue prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The commandery covered the Zhejiang coast.〉

sentences.zh: 莎車國、鄯善國遣使奉獻。

translations.literal: The states of Suoju and Shanshan sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Suoju and Shanshan in the Western Regions sent missions east.

sentences.zh: 〈莎車、鄯善，並西域國名。

translations.literal: 〈Suoju and Shanshan are both names of Western Region states.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Both lay on the southern Tarim rim.〉

sentences.zh: 鄯音市戰反。〉

translations.literal: Shan is pronounced shi zhan (fanqie).〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The character shan is read shi–zhan.〉

sentences.zh: 十二月癸卯，詔益、涼二州奴婢，自八年以來自訟在所官，一切免為庶民，賣者無還直。

translations.literal: On guimao in the twelfth month an edict said: As for slaves and maidservants in Yi and Liang provinces who since the eighth year have petitioned at the office where they are, all without exception are freed as commoners; sellers need not return the purchase price.
translations.idiomatic: On guimao another edict freed Yi and Liang bondsmen who had sued locally since the eighth year and waived restitution to former owners.

sentences.zh: 十五年春正月辛丑，大司徒韓歆免，自殺。

translations.literal: In the fifteenth year, on xinchou in the first month of spring, grand minister of education Han Xin was dismissed and killed himself.
translations.idiomatic: Han Xin lost the grand minister of education post on xinchou and took his own life.

sentences.zh: 〈事見侯霸傳。〉

translations.literal: 〈The affair is treated in Hou Ba’s biography.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Details appear in the biography of Hou Ba.〉

sentences.zh: 丁未，有星孛於昴。

translations.literal: On dingwei there was a broom star in the Mao asterism.
translations.idiomatic: A comet appeared in Mao on dingwei.

sentences.zh: 汝南太守歐陽歙為大司徒。

translations.literal: Grand administrator of Runan Ouyang She became grand minister of education.
translations.idiomatic: Ouyang She of Runan succeeded Han Xin as grand minister of education.

sentences.zh: 建義大將軍朱祐罷。

translations.literal: Grand general who establishes righteousness Zhu You was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu You left the post grand general who establishes righteousness.

sentences.zh: 丁未，有星孛於營室。

translations.literal: On dingwei there was a broom star in Yingshi.
translations.idiomatic: Another comet blazed in the Yingshi mansion the same cyclical day.

sentences.zh: 二月，徙鴈門、代郡、上谷三郡民，置常關、居庸關以東。

translations.literal: In the second month the people of Yanmen, Dai, and Shanggu commanderies were moved and settled east of the passes at Chang and Juyong.
translations.idiomatic: Three northern commanderies were evacuated south of the Yan wall passes at Changshan barrier and Juyong.

sentences.zh: 〈前書曰代郡有常山關，上谷郡居庸縣有關。

translations.literal: 〈The Former Han says that Dai commandery had Mount Chang barrier and that Juyong county in Shanggu had a barrier pass.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The note cites Former Han geography for the two famous gates.〉

sentences.zh: 時胡寇數犯邊，故徙之。〉

translations.literal: At the time Hu raiders repeatedly violated the border, hence they were moved.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Steppe pressure made the relocation a military necessity.〉

sentences.zh: 初，巴蜀旣平，大司馬吳漢上書請封皇子，不許，重奏連歲。

translations.literal: At first, once Ba and Shu had been pacified, great marshal Wu Han submitted a memorial asking to enfeoff the imperial sons; it was not granted, and he submitted again year after year.
translations.idiomatic: After Shu fell Wu Han repeatedly begged the throne to invest the princes; the emperor long refused.

sentences.zh: 三月，乃詔羣臣議。

translations.literal: In the third month he then ordered the ministers to deliberate.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month he finally told the bureaucracy to debate the matter.

sentences.zh: 大司空融、固始侯通、膠東侯復、高密侯禹、太常登等奏議曰：「古者封建諸侯，以藩屏京師。

translations.literal: Grand minister of works Rong, Marquis of Gushi Tong, Marquis of Jiaodong Fu, Marquis of Gaomi Yu, Minister of Ceremonial Deng, and others memorialized: “In antiquity enfeoffment of the feudal lords was to fence and screen the capital.”
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong, Li Tong, Liu Fu, Deng Yu, the minister of ceremonial Deng, and others argued that classical kingship used feudal shields for the capital.

sentences.zh: 〈藩，蘺也。

translations.literal: 〈Fan means hedge.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Fan meant a hedge,〉

sentences.zh: 屏，蔽也。

translations.literal: ping means screen.
translations.idiomatic: 〉ping a screen before the throne.〉

sentences.zh: 詩大雅曰：「介人維藩，大邦維屏。

translations.literal: The Da ya of the Poetry says: “The good men are the hedge, the great states are the screen.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Shijing line praises kin and great lords as bulwarks.〉

sentences.zh: 」毛萇注曰：「當用公卿諸侯為藩屏也。

translations.literal: ’Mao Chang’s note says: “One should use dukes, ministers, and feudal lords as hedge and screen.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉Mao Chang read the verse as a charter for collateral kingdoms.〉

sentences.zh: 」公羊傳曰：「京者何？

translations.literal: ’The Gongyang zhuan says: “What is jing (here written 京)?
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Gongyang commentary turns to the word jing in the phrase for the capital.〉

sentences.zh: 大也。

translations.literal: It means great.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The gloss answers: “great.”〉

sentences.zh: 師者何？

translations.literal: What is shi (here written 師)?
translations.idiomatic: 〉It next asks about shi in the same binome.〉

sentences.zh: 衆也。

translations.literal: It means the host or multitude.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Shi means the assembled multitude.〉

sentences.zh: 天子之居，必有衆大之辭言之。」〉

translations.literal: The dwelling of the Son of Heaven must be spoken of with a word that is numerous and great.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉So the royal seat must be named with language that conveys both vastness and multitude.”〉

sentences.zh: 周封八百，

translations.literal: The Zhou enfeoffed eight hundred.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou invested some eight hundred lords.

sentences.zh: 〈史記曰「唐、虞協和萬國，逮于夏、商，或數千，蓋周封八百」也。〉

translations.literal: 〈The Shiji says: “Tang and Yu harmonized the myriad states; down to Xia and Shang they numbered in the thousands; Zhou enfeoffed about eight hundred.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Shiji contrasts mythic myriads with Zhou’s hundreds.〉

sentences.zh: 同姓諸姬並為建國，

translations.literal: The various Ji of the same surname together became established states,
translations.idiomatic: Zhou kin of the Ji surname held many of those states,

sentences.zh: 〈左傳曰：「虞、虢、焦、滑、霍、楊、韓、魏，皆姬姓也。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Zuozhuan says: “Yu, Guo, Jiao, Huo, Han, Wei, and Yang were all of the Ji surname.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Zuozhuan lists major Ji polities along the Yellow River.〉

sentences.zh: 夾輔王室，尊事天子，享國永長，為後世法。

translations.literal: flanking and aiding the royal house, honoring and serving the Son of Heaven, enjoying their states for long ages, and becoming a model for later ages.
translations.idiomatic: and they propped the king while honoring the Zhou Son of Heaven for generations—a pattern later ages copied.

sentences.zh: 故詩云：『大啟爾宇，為周室輔。』

translations.literal: Therefore the Poetry says: “Greatly open your demesne, be a bulwark for the house of Zhou.”
translations.idiomatic: The ode urges a great lord to widen his domain as Zhou’s prop.

sentences.zh: 〈詩魯頌也。

translations.literal: 〈It is an ode of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The line comes from the Lu hymns.〉

sentences.zh: 宇，居也。

translations.literal: Yu means dwelling.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Yu means territory or seat.〉

sentences.zh: 周成王封周公子伯禽於魯。

translations.literal: King Cheng of Zhou enfeoffed the Duke of Zhou’s son Boji in Lu.
translations.idiomatic: 〉King Cheng enfeoffed Boji at Lu as the Zhou uncle’s heir.〉

sentences.zh: 言大開爾居，以為我周家之輔。〉

translations.literal: It says greatly to open your dwelling so as to be a helper to our Zhou house.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The gloss paraphrases the blessing on the Lu founder.〉

sentences.zh: 高祖聖德，光有天下，亦務親親，封立兄弟諸子，不違舊章。

translations.literal: The High Ancestor, in his sagely virtue, spread his light over the realm; he also devoted himself to loving kin, enfeoffing brothers and sons, and did not violate the old pattern.
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu likewise enfeoffed brothers and sons without breaking classical precedent.

sentences.zh: 陛下德橫天地，興復宗統，襃德賞勳，親睦九族，

translations.literal: Your Majesty’s virtue spans heaven and earth; you have raised anew the ancestral line, honored merit and rewarded achievement, and brought loving harmony to the nine kindreds,
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu had restored the Liu house, heaped rewards on kin and generals, and knit the nine agnates in harmony,

sentences.zh: 〈孔安國注尚書云：「九族謂上至高祖，下至玄孫。」〉

translations.literal: 〈Kong Anguo’s note on the Documents says: “The nine kindreds means from the high ancestor down to the great-great-grandson.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉“Nine kindreds” spans five generations of ascent and descent from ego.〉

sentences.zh: 功臣宗室，咸蒙封爵，多受廣地，或連屬縣。

translations.literal: Meritorious subjects and the imperial clan alike have all received enfeoffment and noble rank; many hold broad territories, some linking several counties.
translations.idiomatic: Generals and Liu kinsmen alike had already been raised to noble rank, often with vast fiefs strung across multiple counties.

sentences.zh: 今皇子賴天，能勝衣趨拜，陛下恭謙克讓，抑而未議，羣臣百姓，莫不失望。

translations.literal: Now the imperial sons, relying on Heaven, are able to wear full robes and perform obeisance; Your Majesty, reverently modest and yielding, has held back and not yet decided—the ministers and commoners are without exception disappointed.
translations.idiomatic: The princes had come of age for court dress and bows, yet Guangwu still deferred investiture, frustrating court and country alike.

sentences.zh: 冝因盛夏吉時，定號位，以廣藩輔，

translations.literal: It is fitting to use the auspicious midsummer season to fix titles and ranks so as to broaden the hedge of assistants,
translations.idiomatic: The memorial urged summer enthronements to give the boys feudal shields around the throne,

sentences.zh: 〈禮記月令：「天子孟夏迎夏於南郊，還，乃封諸侯，行爵出祿。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Liji, month of summer: “The Son of Heaven welcomes summer at the southern suburb, returns, then enfeoffs the feudal lords, conferring ranks and issuing stipends.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Yueling prescribed midsummer as the classical moment for such grants.〉

sentences.zh: 明親親，尊宗廟，重社稷，應古合舊，厭塞衆心。

translations.literal: clarifying love of kin, honoring the ancestral shrines, giving weight to altars of soil and grain, answering antiquity and matching the old pattern, and satisfying the hearts of the multitude.
translations.idiomatic: Investiture would display love of kin, dignify the temples, and steady the realm in line with ancient precedent.

sentences.zh: 臣請大司空上輿地圖，

translations.literal: Your servant begs the grand minister of works to submit the cartographic maps of the realm,
translations.idiomatic: They asked Dou Rong to forward territorial maps,

sentences.zh: 〈廣雅曰：「輿，載也。

translations.literal: 〈The Guangya says: “Yu means to carry.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉Yu meant what the earth bears.〉

sentences.zh: 」言載在地者，皆圖畫之。

translations.literal: It means that whatever rests on the earth is all depicted in maps.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Every feature on the ground was to be charted.〉

sentences.zh: 司空掌土地，故命上之。〉

translations.literal: The minister of works supervises land, hence he is ordered to submit them.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The office’s portfolio made Dou Rong the right source.〉

sentences.zh: 太常擇吉日，具禮儀。

translations.literal: Let the minister of ceremonial choose an auspicious day and prepare the full ritual.”
translations.idiomatic: The minister of ceremonial was to pick the day and set the liturgy.”

translations.literal: An edict said: “Granted.”
translations.idiomatic: The emperor answered simply: “Approved.”

sentences.zh: 夏四月戊申，以太牢告祠宗廟。

translations.literal: In the fourth month of summer, on wushen, the grand sacrifice (tai lao) was announced at the ancestral shrines.
translations.idiomatic: On wushen in the fourth month he reported the investitures to the Liu shrines with a tai lao offering.

sentences.zh: 丁巳，使大司空融告廟，封皇子輔為右翊公，英為楚公，陽為東海公，康為濟南公，蒼為東平公，延為淮陽公，荊為山陽公，衡為臨淮公，焉為左翊公，京為琅邪公。

translations.literal: On dingsi he sent great minister of works Rong to announce at the shrines and enfeoff the imperial sons: Fu as duke of Youyi, Ying as duke of Chu, Yang as duke of Donghai, Kang as duke of Jinan, Cang as duke of Dongping, Yan as duke of Huaiyang, Jing as duke of Shanyang, Heng as duke of Linhuai, Yan as duke of Zuoyi, and Jing as duke of Langye.
translations.idiomatic: On dingsi Dou Rong proclaimed at the temples and ten princes received ducal titles from Fu through Jing of Langye.

sentences.zh: 癸丑，追謚兄伯升為齊武公，兄仲為魯哀公。

translations.literal: On guichou posthumous titles were granted: elder brother Bosheng was titled martial duke of Qi, elder brother Zhong was titled lamented duke of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Guichou brought posthumous honors for Liu Bosheng and Liu Zhong.

sentences.zh: 六月庚午，復置屯騎、長水、射聲三校尉官；

translations.literal: On gengwu in the sixth month the three colonelcies of tun qi, chang shui, and she sheng were re-established;
translations.idiomatic: The metropolitan guard colonelcies abolished in the seventh year were restored on gengwu in the sixth month;

sentences.zh: 〈七年罷。〉

translations.literal: 〈They had been abolished in the seventh year.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The note recalls the earlier cut.〉

sentences.zh: 改青巾左校尉為越騎校尉。

translations.literal: the colonel of the left with the green kerchief was renamed colonel of the Yue cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: The green-kerchief left colonel became the Yue cavalry colonel instead.

sentences.zh: 詔下州郡檢覈墾田頃［］

translations.literal: An edict ordered commanderies and provinces to inspect and verify opened field acreage
translations.idiomatic: Provinces were ordered to audit reclaimed acreage

sentences.zh: 〈墾，闢也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Ken means to open up.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Ken meant newly broken ground.〉

sentences.zh: 及戶口年紀，又考實二千石長吏阿枉不平者。

translations.literal: and household registers with ages, and also to investigate and verify any magistrates at two thousand shi who bend the law unfairly.
translations.idiomatic: together with population rolls and ages, and to expose corrupt two-thousand-shi magistrates.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月甲戌，大司徒歐陽歙下獄死。

translations.literal: On jiaxu in the eleventh month of winter grand minister of education Ouyang She was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Ouyang She died in prison as grand minister of education on jiaxu in winter.

sentences.zh: 十二月庚午，關內侯戴涉為大司徒。

translations.literal: On gengwu in the twelfth month Guannei marquis Dai She became grand minister of education.
translations.idiomatic: Dai She succeeded him on gengwu in the twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 盧芳自匈奴入居高柳。

translations.literal: Lu Fang came in from the Xiongnu and occupied Gaoliu.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Fang slipped back from the steppe and held Gaoliu.

sentences.zh: 是歲，驃騎大將軍杜茂免。

translations.literal: That year grand general of agile cavalry Du Mao was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Du Mao lost his post as grand general of agile cavalry.

sentences.zh: 虎牙大將軍蓋延薨。

translations.literal: Grand general Gai Yan styled Huya died.
translations.idiomatic: Huya general Gai Yan died.

sentences.zh: 十六年春二月，交阯女子徵側反，略有城邑。

translations.literal: In the sixteenth year, in the second month of spring, the woman Zheng Ce of Jiaozhi rebelled and overran towns.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixteenth year’s second month Zheng Ce of Jiaozhi rose and seized urban centers.

sentences.zh: 三月辛丑晦，日有蝕之。

translations.literal: On xinchou, the last day of the third month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The third month closed on xinchou with an eclipse.

sentences.zh: 秋九月，河南尹張伋及諸郡守十餘人，坐度田不實，皆下獄死。

translations.literal: In the ninth month of autumn grand administrator of Henan Zhang Ji and more than ten other commandery governors, because their land surveys were untrue, were all imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Henan governor Zhang Ji and a dozen peers were executed for falsifying cadastral returns that autumn.

sentences.zh: 〈東觀記曰：「刺史太守多為詐巧，不務實核，苟以度田為名，聚人田中，並度廬屋里落，聚人遮道啼呼。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Dong guan ji says: “Inspectors and grand administrators mostly practiced deceit and cleverness, not bothering with real verification; under the name of measuring fields they crowded people in the paddies, also measuring huts and hamlets, while crowds blocked the roads weeping.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Local officials had abused the survey to harass households.〉

sentences.zh: 郡國大姓及兵長、羣盜處處並起，攻劫在所，害殺長吏。

translations.literal: Great clans of commanderies and kingdoms, militia leaders, and bandit gangs rose everywhere together, raiding wherever they were, killing chief magistrates.
translations.idiomatic: Powerful lineages and band chiefs rose in concert, murdering magistrates across the provinces.

sentences.zh: 郡縣追討，到則解散，去復屯結。

translations.literal: When commanderies and counties pursued and attacked them, they dispersed on arrival, then regathered when the troops left.
translations.idiomatic: Government columns dispersed the gangs on contact only to see them re-form.

sentences.zh: 青、徐、幽、兾四州尤甚。

translations.literal: It was worst in Qing, Xu, You, and Ji provinces.
translations.idiomatic: Qing, Xu, You, and Ji suffered most severely.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，遣使者下郡國，聽羣盜自相糾擿，

translations.literal: In the tenth month of winter envoys were sent down to commanderies and kingdoms, allowing bandit bands to inform on one another,
translations.idiomatic: Winter envoys authorized bandits to denounce one another,

sentences.zh: 〈擿猶發也。

translations.literal: 〈Di means to expose;
translations.idiomatic: 〉Di meant to expose or inform.〉

sentences.zh: 音它狄反。〉

translations.literal: pronounced ta di (fanqie).〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The gloss gives ta–di.〉

sentences.zh: 五人共斬一人者，除其罪。

translations.literal: if five men jointly behead one man, their guilt is remitted.
translations.idiomatic: a quintet that produced one bandit’s head won amnesty.

sentences.zh: 吏雖逗留回避故縱者，皆勿問，聽以禽討為效。

translations.literal: Clerks who tarry, evade, or deliberately let them go are all not questioned; only taking capture of bandits as the measure of effect.
translations.idiomatic: Minor officials were not punished for slow pursuit if captures still resulted.

sentences.zh: 其牧守令長坐界內盜賊而不收捕者，又以畏愞捐城委守者，皆不以為負，

translations.literal: As for shepherds and grand administrators and magistrates guilty because bandits within their borders were not seized, and also those who from cowardice abandoned cities and cast aside defense, all are not held liable,
translations.idiomatic: Magistrates who lost towns to timidity were not automatically condemned,

sentences.zh: 〈委守謂棄其所守也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Wei shou means abandoning what they guarded.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Wei shou meant deserting a post one was charged to hold.〉

sentences.zh: 但取獲賊多少為殿最，

translations.literal: but only the number of bandits captured is taken for ranking last or first,
translations.idiomatic: rankings rested solely on body counts of captured rebels,

sentences.zh: 〈殿，後也。

translations.literal: 〈Dian means “behind” in ranking;
translations.idiomatic: 〉Dian in assessment jargon meant ranking last among magistrates.〉

sentences.zh: 謂課居後也。

translations.literal: it means ranking last in the assessment.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The lowest tier of annual review.〉

sentences.zh: 最，凡要之首也。

translations.literal: Zui means the top or head of the reckoning;
translations.idiomatic: 〉Zui meant leading the performance list for captures.〉

sentences.zh: 言課居先也。〉

translations.literal: it means ranking first in the assessment.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Magistrates with the most captures led the list.〉

sentences.zh: 唯蔽匿者乃罪之。

translations.literal: only those who conceal them are then punished.
translations.idiomatic: Only officials who hid bandits faced charges.

sentences.zh: 於是更相追捕，賊並解散。

translations.literal: Thereupon they pursued one another in turn, and the bandits all dispersed.
translations.idiomatic: Mutual denunciation and pursuit broke the uprisings apart.

sentences.zh: 徙其魁帥於它郡，賦田受稟，使安生業。

translations.literal: Their chieftains were moved to other commanderies, granted fields, and issued rations so they could settle to lawful livelihood.
translations.idiomatic: Ringleaders were deported, given land and grain, and settled as farmers.

sentences.zh: 自是牛馬放牧，邑門不閉。

translations.literal: From this cattle and horses pastured freely and city gates were not closed.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward the countryside was calm enough to leave gates unbarred.

sentences.zh: 盧芳遣使乞降。

translations.literal: Lu Fang sent envoys begging to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Fang sued for peace through envoys.

sentences.zh: 十二月甲辰，封芳為代王。

translations.literal: On jiachen in the twelfth month Fang was enfeoffed as king of Dai.
translations.idiomatic: On jiachen he received the king of Dai seal as the price of submission.

sentences.zh: 初，王莽亂後，貨幣雜用布、帛、金、粟。

translations.literal: At first, after Wang Mang’s turmoil, currency was mixed in use—cloth, silk, gold, and grain.
translations.idiomatic: After Wang Mang, barter and odd media had circulated alongside coin.

sentences.zh: 是歲，始行五銖錢。

translations.literal: That year the wu zhu cash was first put in circulation.
translations.idiomatic: The state reintroduced standard wu zhu coinage that year.

sentences.zh: 〈武帝始為五銖錢，王莽時廢，今始行之。〉

translations.literal: 〈Emperor Wu first cast wu zhu; Wang Mang abolished it; now it is first issued again.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Emperor Wu’s coin returned after Mang’s experiments.〉

sentences.zh: 十七年春正月，趙公良薨。

translations.literal: In the seventeenth year, in the first month of spring, Duke Liang of Zhao died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Liang, duke of Zhao, died in the first month of the seventeenth year.

sentences.zh: 二月乙亥晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On yihai, the last day of the second month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: An eclipse closed the second month on yihai.

sentences.zh: 〈東觀記曰：「上以日食避正殿，讀圖讖多，御坐廡下淺露，中風發疾，苦眩甚。

translations.literal: 〈The Dong guan ji says: “Because of the eclipse the emperor avoided the main hall; he read prognostic texts so much that sitting in the corridor under the eaves he caught a chill, fell ill with severe dizziness.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The emperor’s eclipse penance led to illness on tour.〉

sentences.zh: 左右有白大司馬史，病苦如此，不能動搖。

translations.literal: Those at his side reported to the grand marshal’s clerk that the illness was so severe he could not be moved.
translations.idiomatic: Aides told the marshal’s office he was too ill to travel.

sentences.zh: 自強從公，出乘，以車行數里，病差。

translations.literal: He forced himself to attend court, rode out in a carriage, and after several li felt somewhat better.
translations.idiomatic: He insisted on riding a few li until the vertigo eased.

sentences.zh: 四月二日，車駕宿偃師。

translations.literal: On the second day of the fourth month the imperial carriage lodged at Yanshi.
translations.idiomatic: He broke journey at Yanshi on the second of the fourth month.

sentences.zh: 病差數日，入南陽界，到葉。

translations.literal: After several days of improvement he entered the borders of Nanyang and reached Ye.
translations.idiomatic: Recovering, he crossed into Nanyang as far as Ye.

sentences.zh: 以車騎省，留數日行，黎陽兵馬千餘匹，遂到章陵，起居平愈。」〉

translations.literal: Because chariot and horse escort was reduced, he stayed several days; more than a thousand cavalry from Liyang arrived, and he reached Zhangling, where his condition became calm again.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉A slim escort brought him slowly home to Zhangling to convalesce.”〉

sentences.zh: 夏四月乙卯，南巡狩，皇太子及右翊公輔、楚公英、東海公陽、濟南公康、東平公蒼從，幸潁川，進幸葉、章陵。

translations.literal: On yimao in the fourth month of summer he toured south; the heir apparent and Dukes Fu, Ying, Yang, Kang, and Cang accompanied; he visited Yingchuan, then advanced to Ye and Zhangling.
translations.idiomatic: The southern progress that summer took the crown prince and five young dukes through Yingchuan to Ye and the imperial tombs.

sentences.zh: 〈葉，縣，故楚葉公邑，屬南陽郡，今許州縣也。

translations.literal: 〈Ye is a county, the old seat of Lord Ye of Chu, in Nanyang commandery; it is now a county in Xu prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Ye lay in southern Nanyang.〉

sentences.zh: 葉音式涉反。〉

translations.literal: Ye is pronounced shi she (fanqie).〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The reading is shi–she.〉

sentences.zh: 五月乙卯，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On yimao in the fifth month the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: He was back in Luoyang on yimao in the fifth month.

sentences.zh: 六月癸巳，臨淮公衡薨。

translations.literal: On guisi in the sixth month Duke Heng of Linhuai died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Heng, duke of Linhuai, died on guisi.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，妖巫李廣等羣起據皖城，

translations.literal: In the seventh month of autumn sorcerers such as Li Guang of Yao rose in a body and seized Wancheng,
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month cult leaders including Li Guang seized Wancheng in Lujiang,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬廬江郡，故城在今舒州，有皖水。

translations.literal: 〈Wancheng is a county in Lujiang commandery; its old walled town is in modern Shu prefecture and has the Wan River.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Wancheng sat on the Wan River in central Anhui.〉

sentences.zh: 音下板反。〉

translations.literal: pronounced xia ban (fanqie).〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The place name is read xia–ban.〉

sentences.zh: 遣虎賁中郎將馬援、驃騎將軍段志討之。

translations.literal: Tiger-rushing leader of court gentlemen Ma Yuan and general of agile cavalry Duan Zhi were sent to campaign against them.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan and Duan Zhi were ordered to retake the town.

sentences.zh: 九月，破皖城，斬李廣等。

translations.literal: In the ninth month they broke Wancheng and beheaded Li Guang and others.
translations.idiomatic: Wancheng fell in the ninth month and Li Guang lost his head.

sentences.zh: 冬十月辛巳，廢皇后郭氏為中山太后，立貴人陰氏為皇后。

translations.literal: On xinsi in the tenth month of winter Empress Guo was deposed as queen and styled queen mother of Zhongshan; Lady Yin the honored consort was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu set aside Empress Guo for Lady Yin that winter, elevating the latter as his new consort.

sentences.zh: 進右翊公輔為中山王，食常山郡。

translations.literal: Duke Fu of Youyi was advanced to king of Zhongshan with revenue from Changshan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Fu became king of Zhongshan with the Changshan tax base.

sentences.zh: 〈本恒山郡，避文帝諱改為常山，故城在今趙州元氏縣西。〉

translations.literal: 〈Originally Hengshan commandery; to avoid Emperor Wen’s taboo it was changed to Changshan; its old walled town is west of Yuanshi county in Zhao prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Changshan had been renamed from Hengshan for Han Wendi’s taboo.〉

sentences.zh: 其餘九國公，皆即舊封進爵為王。

translations.literal: The remaining nine ducal states were all advanced from their old fiefs to the rank of king.
translations.idiomatic: The other nine royal sons were promoted from duke to king on their existing domains.

sentences.zh: 甲申，幸章陵。

translations.literal: On jiashen he visited Zhangling.
translations.idiomatic: He visited Zhangling again on jiashen.

sentences.zh: 脩園廟，祠舊宅，觀田廬，置酒作樂，賞賜。

translations.literal: He repaired the park shrines, sacrificed at the old residence, inspected fields and cottages, set out wine and music, and distributed rewards.
translations.idiomatic: He toured the family estate, feasted kinsmen, and handed out gifts.

sentences.zh: 時宗室諸母因酣恱，相與語曰：「文叔少時謹信，與人不款曲，唯直柔耳。

translations.literal: At the time imperial clan aunts, flushed with wine, said to one another: “Wenshu in youth was careful and trustworthy; with people he was not ingratiating—only straightforward and yielding.
translations.idiomatic: Tipsy aunts recalled young Liu Xiu as blunt and modest,

sentences.zh: 今乃能如此！

translations.literal: Now he is able to be like this!
translations.idiomatic: yet now he ruled like a sage.

sentences.zh: 」帝聞之，大笑曰：「吾理天下，亦欲以柔道行之。

translations.literal: ”When the emperor heard it, he laughed greatly and said: “In ordering the realm I also intend to practice the way of yielding.”
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu overheard and laughed that he still meant to rule with softness.”

sentences.zh: 」乃悉為舂陵宗室起祠堂。

translations.literal: ’Thereupon he had shrines built for all the Zhangling lineage.
translations.idiomatic: He then commissioned ancestral halls for every Zhangling branch.

sentences.zh: 有五鳳皇見於潁川之郟縣。

translations.literal: Five phoenixes appeared in Jia county of Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: Five phoenixes were sighted at Jia in Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 〈郟，今汝州郟城縣也。

translations.literal: 〈Jia is now Jiacheng county in Ru prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The place lies south of the Song uplands.〉

sentences.zh: 東觀記曰：「鳳高八尺，五彩，羣鳥並從，行列蓋地數頃，停一十七日。」〉

translations.literal: The Dong guan ji says: “The birds stood eight chi tall, in five colors; flocks of other birds followed; their ranks covered several qing of ground and they halted for seventeen days.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Witnesses claimed an enormous multicolored flock lingered more than two weeks.〉

sentences.zh: 十二月，至自章陵。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month he returned from Zhangling.
translations.idiomatic: He completed the Zhangling circuit in the twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 是歲，莎車國遣使貢獻。

translations.literal: That year the state of Suoju sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Suoju again sent a mission westward.

sentences.zh: 十八年春二月，蜀郡守將史歆叛，遣大司馬吳漢率二將軍討之，圍成都。

translations.literal: In the eighteenth year, in the second month of spring, Shi Xin, garrison commander of Shu commandery, rebelled; great marshal Wu Han leading two other generals was sent to campaign against him and besieged Chengdu.
translations.idiomatic: Shi Xin’s mutiny in Shu drew Wu Han back to invest Chengdu in the eighteenth year’s second month.

sentences.zh: 甲寅，西廵狩，幸長安。

translations.literal: On jiayin he toured west and visited Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin he began a western progress to Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 三月壬午，祠高廟，遂有事十一陵。

translations.literal: On renwu in the third month he sacrificed at the lofty temple, then performed the rites at the eleven imperial tombs.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu in the third month he worshipped at Gaozu’s temple and the Western Han tombs.

sentences.zh: 歷馮翊界，進幸蒲坂，祠后土。

translations.literal: Passing through the borders of Pingyi, he advanced to Puban and sacrificed to Hou tu.
translations.idiomatic: He crossed Pingyi and offered to the earth goddess at Puban.

sentences.zh: 〈漢官儀曰：「祭地於河東汾陰后土宮。

translations.literal: 〈The Han guan yi says: “Earth was sacrificed at the Hou tu palace in Fenyin of Hedong.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The suburban earth altar sat on a square mound in the bend of the Fen.〉

sentences.zh: 宮曲入河，古之祭地，澤中方丘也。

translations.literal: The palace curved into the river; the ancient sacrifice to earth was the square mound in the marsh.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The rite followed classical square-mound geography.〉

sentences.zh: 以夏至日祭，其禮儀如祭天。

translations.literal: On the summer solstice day it was sacrificed to; its ritual forms were like sacrifice to Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Summer solstice matched the winter solstice rites to heaven.〉

sentences.zh: 」蒲阪，縣，屬河東郡。

translations.literal: ’Puban is a county in Hedong commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Puban county hosted the crossing.

sentences.zh: 后土祠在今蒲州汾陰縣西北。〉

translations.literal: The Hou tu shrine is northwest of Fenyin county in Pu prefecture today.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The modern site is noted for travelers.〉

sentences.zh: 夏四月甲戌車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On jiaxu in the fourth month of summer the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: He was home on jiaxu in the fourth month.

sentences.zh: 癸酉，詔曰：「今邊郡盜穀五十斛，罪至於死，開殘吏妄殺之路，其蠲除此法，同之內郡。」

translations.literal: On guiyou an edict said: “Now in border commanderies stealing fifty hu of grain is a crime reaching death, opening a road for cruel clerks to kill recklessly; let that law be repealed and made the same as inner commanderies.”
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou an edict repealed the border rule that theft of fifty hu of grain carried the death penalty, aligning the statute with the milder law in the interior commanderies.

sentences.zh: 遣伏波將軍馬援率樓船將軍段志等擊交阯賊徵側等。

translations.literal: He sent wave-quelling general Ma Yuan leading tower-ship general Duan Zhi and others to strike the Jiaozhi bandits Zheng Ce and her followers.
translations.idiomatic: He dispatched Ma Yuan as wave-quelling general with Duan Zhi as tower-ship general to crush the Jiaozhi rising led by Zheng Ce.

sentences.zh: 戊申，幸河內。

translations.literal: On wushen he visited Henei.
translations.idiomatic: On wushen he toured Henei.

sentences.zh: 戊子，至自河內。

translations.literal: On wuzi he returned from Henei.
translations.idiomatic: He came back from Henei on wuzi.

sentences.zh: 五月，旱。

translations.literal: In the fifth month there was drought.
translations.idiomatic: Drought gripped the fifth month.

sentences.zh: 盧芳復亡入匈奴。

translations.literal: Lu Fang again fled and entered the Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Fang broke faith once more and rode back to the northern confederation.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，吳漢拔成都，斬史歆等。

translations.literal: In the seventh month of autumn Wu Han took Chengdu and beheaded Shi Xin and others.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han stormed Chengdu that seventh month and executed Shi Xin’s faction.

sentences.zh: 壬戌，赦益州所部殊死已下。

translations.literal: On renxu he amnestied all under death by dismemberment in the territory subject to Yi province.
translations.idiomatic: On renxu a general pardon swept Yi commandery lands short of the worst capital crimes.

sentences.zh: 冬十月庚辰，幸冝城。

translations.literal: On gengchen in the tenth month of winter he visited Yicheng.
translations.idiomatic: He toured Yicheng in Nan commandery on gengchen in the tenth month.

sentences.zh: 〈縣，屬南郡，楚之鄢邑也，故城在今襄州率道縣南。〉

translations.literal: 〈It is a county in Nan commandery, the old Yan settlement of Chu; its old walled town is south of Shuidao county in Xiang prefecture today.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Yicheng was the old Chu stronghold south of the Han middle Yangzi.〉

sentences.zh: 還，祠章陵。

translations.literal: Returning, he sacrificed at Zhangling.
translations.idiomatic: On the way back he offered again at the Zhangling shrines.

sentences.zh: 十二月乙丑，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On yichou in the twelfth month the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: He reached Luoyang on yichou in the twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 是歲，罷州牧，置刺史。

translations.literal: That year the provincial shepherd was abolished and inspectors were established.
translations.idiomatic: Provincial governors were demoted back to the old inspector system.

sentences.zh: 〈武帝元封五年初置部刺史，掌奉詔條察州，秩六百石，員十三人。

translations.literal: 〈In the fifth year of Yuanfeng Emperor Wu first established regional inspectors, who held the edict articles and inspected provinces, rank six hundred shi, thirteen posts.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Emperor Wu’s thirteen regional inspectors had been the original model.〉

sentences.zh: 成帝綏和元年更名牧，秩二千石。

translations.literal: In the first year of Suihe of Emperor Cheng the title was changed to shepherd, rank two thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Chengdi raised them to shepherds at two thousand shi.〉

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平二年復為刺史，元壽二年復為牧。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianping of Emperor Ai they were again inspectors; in the second year of Yuanshou again shepherds.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Aidi toggled the titles twice more.〉

sentences.zh: 經王莽變革，至建武元年復置牧，今改置刺史。〉

translations.literal: Through Wang Mang’s changes, down to the first year of Jianwu shepherds were again set up; now they are changed back to inspectors.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Guangwu had briefly restored shepherds; he now reverted to inspectors.〉

sentences.zh: 十九年春正月庚子，追尊孝宣皇帝曰中宗。

translations.literal: In the nineteenth year, on gengzi in the first month of spring, filial Emperor Xuan was posthumously honored as Zhongzong.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Xuan received the temple name Zhongzong on gengzi in the first month of the nineteenth year.

sentences.zh: 始祠昭帝、元帝於太廟，

translations.literal: He began to sacrifice to Emperor Zhao and Emperor Yuan in the Grand Temple,
translations.idiomatic: Zhao and Yuan were admitted to the great shrine alongside the main line,

sentences.zh: 〈漢官儀曰：「光武第雖十二，於父子之次，於成帝為兄弟，於哀帝為諸父，於平帝為祖父，皆不可為之後。

translations.literal: 〈The Han guan yi says: “Although Guangwu’s generational rank is twelfth, in the order of father and son he is a brother to Emperor Cheng, an uncle to Emperor Ai, and a grandfather to Emperor Ping—none of whom can be his ritual heir.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Guangwu could not continue Ping’s or Cheng’s line.〉

sentences.zh: 上至元帝，於光武為父，故上繼元帝而為九代。

translations.literal: Going up to Emperor Yuan, for Guangwu he is father, hence he succeeds Yuan and counts as the ninth generation.
translations.idiomatic: 〉He therefore continued the count from Emperor Yuan as ninth in descent.〉

sentences.zh: 故河圖云『赤九會昌』，謂光武也。

translations.literal: Therefore the Hetu says ‘The red ninth will flourish,’ meaning Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The prophecy “red nine” pointed to him as ninth from the red Han founder.〉

sentences.zh: 」然則宣帝為祖，故追尊及祠之。〉

translations.literal: ”Thus Emperor Xuan is his ancestor, hence he is posthumously honored and sacrificed to.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Xuan therefore entered his ancestral pantheon as grandfather.〉

sentences.zh: 成帝、哀帝、平帝於長安，舂陵節侯以下四世於章陵。

translations.literal: Emperor Cheng, Emperor Ai, and Emperor Ping are at Chang’an; four generations below Marquis Jie of Chunling are at Zhangling.
translations.idiomatic: Western sovereigns stayed enshrined at Chang’an while four Chunling generations received cult at Zhangling.

sentences.zh: 妖巫單臣、傅鎮等反，據原武，遣太中大夫臧宮圍之。

translations.literal: The sorcerers Shan Chen, Fu Zhen, and others rebelled, seized Yuanwu, and grand counselor of the palace Zang Gong was sent to besiege them.
translations.idiomatic: Shan Chen and Fu Zhen seized Yuanwu until Zang Gong invested the town.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，拔原武，斬臣、鎮等。

translations.literal: In the fourth month of summer Yuanwu was taken and Chen, Zhen, and others were beheaded.
translations.idiomatic: The fourth month brought the storming of Yuanwu and the leaders’ execution.

sentences.zh: 伏波將軍馬援破交阯，斬徵側等。

translations.literal: Wave-quelling general Ma Yuan defeated Jiaozhi and beheaded Zheng Ce and others.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan ended the Jiaozhi rebellion and killed Zheng Ce.

sentences.zh: 因擊破九真賊都陽等，降之。

translations.literal: He then attacked and broke the bandits Du Yang and others in Jiuzhen and accepted their surrender.
translations.idiomatic: He followed up in Jiuzhen against Du Yang’s band and received their capitulation.

sentences.zh: 閏月戊申，進趙、齊、魯三國公爵為王。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month, on wushen, the ducal ranks of Zhao, Qi, and Lu were advanced to king.
translations.idiomatic: On wushen in the intercalary month three royal sons were promoted from duke to king of Zhao, Qi, and Lu.

sentences.zh: 六月戊申，詔曰：「春秋之義，立子以貴。

translations.literal: On wushen in the sixth month an edict said: “By the meaning of the Chunqiu, when establishing a son one goes by eminence of birth.”
translations.idiomatic: On wushen in the sixth month he cited Gongyang doctrine: the chosen heir follows the mother’s rank,

sentences.zh: 〈公羊傳曰：「立嫡以長不以賢，立子以貴不以長。

translations.literal: 〈The Gongyang zhuan says: “In establishing the primary heir one goes by seniority of age, not worthiness; in establishing a son one goes by eminence of birth, not seniority of age.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The canonical rule favored the son of the chief consort.〉

sentences.zh: 桓公何以貴？

translations.literal: Why was Duke Huan esteemed?
translations.idiomatic: 〉The text asks why Duke Huan ranked high.〉

sentences.zh: 母貴也。

translations.literal: Because the mother was esteemed.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Because his mother was of high rank.〉

sentences.zh: 母貴則子貴，子以母貴，母以子貴。」〉

translations.literal: When the mother is esteemed the son is esteemed; the son is esteemed because of the mother, the mother is esteemed because of the son.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Mother and son mutually elevated each other’s ritual standing.”〉

sentences.zh: 東海王陽，皇后之子，冝承大統。

translations.literal: King Yang of Donghai is the son of the empress; he ought to inherit the great line.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yang, born of Empress Yin, was fit to succeed the throne.

sentences.zh: 皇太子彊，崇執謙退，願備藩國。

translations.literal: The heir apparent Qiang, holding modestly to yielding, wishes to serve in a feudal kingdom.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Qiang had repeatedly asked to step down to a kingdom.

sentences.zh: 父子之情，重乆違之。

translations.literal: The feeling between father and son grieves at long separation.
translations.idiomatic: The father-son bond made the split painful,

sentences.zh: 其以彊為東海王，立陽為皇太子，改名莊。」

translations.literal: let Qiang be made king of Donghai, Yang be established as heir apparent, changing his name to Zhuang.”
translations.idiomatic: yet Liu Qiang was named king of Donghai while Liu Yang became heir apparent Zhuang.”

sentences.zh: 秋九月，南巡狩。

translations.literal: In the ninth month of autumn he toured south.
translations.idiomatic: He opened a southern progress that autumn.

sentences.zh: 壬申，幸南陽，進幸汝南南頓縣舍，置酒會，賜吏人，復南頓田租歲。

translations.literal: On renshen he visited Nanyang, advanced to the county lodging of Nandun in Runan, set out a banquet, bestowed gifts on clerks and people, and remitted the field rent of Nandun for one year.
translations.idiomatic: At Nandun he feasted locals and waived a year’s rent on the town where his father had lived.

sentences.zh: 父老前叩頭言：「皇考居此日乆，陛下識知寺舍，

translations.literal: The elders came forward, knocked their heads, and said: “The late emperor dwelt here long; Your Majesty recognizes the official lodgings,
translations.idiomatic: Elders begged for a longer remission, saying Guangwu knew the old yamen from boyhood,

sentences.zh: 〈蔡邕獨斷曰：「陛，階陛也。

translations.literal: 〈Cai Yong’s Du duan says: “Bi means the palace steps.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Bi denoted the palace steps one dared not name directly.〉

sentences.zh: 與天子言不敢指斥，故云陛下。

translations.literal: In speaking with the Son of Heaven one does not dare point and name him, hence one says ‘Your Majesty.’
translations.idiomatic: 〉Hence courtiers said “bixia” instead of naming the ruler.〉

sentences.zh: 」風俗通曰：「寺，司也。

translations.literal: ”The Fengsu tong says: “Si means to administer.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Si (in sisi) meant government bureaus.〉

sentences.zh: 諸官府所止皆曰寺。

translations.literal: Every place where offices stop is called si.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Any official compound could be called a si.〉

sentences.zh: 」光武甞從皇考至南頓，故識知官府舍宇。〉

translations.literal: ”Guangwu once followed the late emperor to Nandun, hence he knows the government buildings.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The emperor really did remember Nandun’s buildings from youth.〉

sentences.zh: 每來輒加厚恩，願賜復十年。

translations.literal: each time you come you add deep grace; we beg a remission of ten years.
translations.idiomatic: They pressed for a decade-long tax holiday.”

sentences.zh: 」帝曰：「天下重器，常恐不任，日復一日，安敢遠期十歲乎？

translations.literal: ”The emperor said: “The realm is a heavy vessel; I always fear I am not equal to it—day after day—how dare I promise ten years in the distance?
translations.idiomatic: He replied that ruling was burden enough without pledging ten years ahead.”

sentences.zh: 」吏人又言：「陛下實惜之，何言謙也？

translations.literal: ”The clerks and people again said: “Your Majesty truly cherishes us; why speak so modestly?
translations.idiomatic: The crowd called his humility excessive.”

sentences.zh: 」帝大笑，復增一歲。

translations.literal: ”The emperor laughed greatly and added one year.
translations.idiomatic: He laughed and granted one more year’s remit.”

sentences.zh: 進幸淮陽、梁、沛。

translations.literal: He advanced and visited Huaiyang, Liang, and Pei.
translations.idiomatic: He then toured Huaiyang, Liang, and Pei.

sentences.zh: 西南夷寇益州郡，

translations.literal: Southwestern Yi raided Yi province commandery,
translations.idiomatic: Tribes southwest struck Yi province commandery,

sentences.zh: 〈常璩華陽國志云：「武帝元封二年叟夷反，將軍郭昌討平之，因開為益州郡。

translations.literal: 〈Chang Qu’s Huayang guo zhi says: “In the second year of Yuanfeng of Emperor Wu the Sou Yi rebelled; general Guo Chang campaigned and pacified them, and it was opened as Yi province commandery.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The commandery dated to Emperor Wu’s conquest of Dian-Sou territory.〉

sentences.zh: 」故城在今昆州晉寧縣是也。〉

translations.literal: ”Its old walled town is what is now Jinning county in Kun prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The seat lay near modern Jinning by Lake Dian.〉

sentences.zh: 遣武威將軍劉尚討之。

translations.literal: He sent general Liu Shang styled Weiwu to campaign against them.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Shang marched as Weiwu general.

sentences.zh: 越巂太守任貴謀叛，十二月，劉尚襲貴，誅之。

translations.literal: Grand administrator of Yuexi Ren Gui plotted rebellion; in the twelfth month Liu Shang attacked Gui and executed him.
translations.idiomatic: Ren Gui’s plot in Yuexi ended when Liu Shang struck in the twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 是歲，復置函谷關都尉。

translations.literal: That year the Hangu pass commandant was re-established.
translations.idiomatic: The Hangu gate warden’s post was revived.

sentences.zh: 〈九年省，今復置。〉

translations.literal: 〈It had been abolished in the ninth year; now it is re-established.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉It had been cut in the ninth year and now returned.〉

sentences.zh: 修西京宮室。

translations.literal: Western capital palace buildings were repaired.
translations.idiomatic: Chang’an palaces underwent repair.

sentences.zh: 二十年春二月戊子，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: In the twentieth year, on wuzi in the second month of spring, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: He was back in Luoyang on wuzi in the twentieth year’s second month.

sentences.zh: 夏四月庚辰，大司徒戴涉下獄死。

translations.literal: On gengchen in the fourth month of summer grand minister of education Dai She was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Dai She died in custody as grand minister of education on gengchen.

sentences.zh: 〈古今注曰：「坐入故太公倉令奚涉罪。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Gu jin zhu says: “He was condemned for covering the crime of former grand granary clerk Xi She.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉He fell for shielding a subordinate’s malfeasance.〉

sentences.zh: 大司空竇融免。

translations.literal: Grand minister of works Dou Rong was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong left the ministry of works.

sentences.zh: 五月辛亥，大司馬吳漢薨。

translations.literal: On xinhai in the fifth month great marshal Wu Han died.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Han died on xinhai in the fifth month.

sentences.zh: 匈奴寇上黨、天水，遂至扶風。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu raided Shangdang and Tianshui and reached as far as You Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: Xiongnu columns pierced to the metropolitan west that year.

sentences.zh: 六月庚寅，廣漢太守蔡茂為大司徒，太僕朱浮為大司空。

translations.literal: On gengyin in the sixth month grand administrator of Guanghan Cai Mao became grand minister of education, grand coachman Zhu Fu grand minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: Cai Mao and Zhu Fu took the two minister posts on gengyin.

sentences.zh: 壬辰，左中郎將劉隆為驃騎將軍，行大司馬事。

translations.literal: On renchen leader of court gentlemen of the left Liu Long became general of agile cavalry and acted in the affairs of great marshal.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Long stepped in as acting great marshal on renchen.

sentences.zh: 〈武帝省太尉，置大司馬將軍；

translations.literal: 〈Emperor Wu abolished grand commandant and set up great marshal as general;
translations.idiomatic: 〉The great marshal began as a military chancellor substitute.〉

sentences.zh: 成帝賜金印紫綬，置官屬，祿比丞相；

translations.literal: Emperor Cheng granted gold seal and purple ribbon, established subordinates, salary like the chancellor;
translations.idiomatic: 〉Chengdi gave it full chancellor-grade staff.〉

sentences.zh: 哀帝去將軍，位在司徒上。

translations.literal: Emperor Ai removed “general”; its rank stood above the minister of education.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Aidi dropped the word “general” but kept precedence over the steward.〉

sentences.zh: 見前書。〉

translations.literal: See the Former Han.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Full pedigree appears in Former Han treatises.〉

sentences.zh: 乙未，徙中山王輔為沛王。

translations.literal: On yiwei King Fu of Zhongshan was moved to be king of Pei.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Fu transferred his kingship from Zhongshan to Pei on yiwei.

sentences.zh: 秋，東夷韓國人率衆詣樂浪內附。

translations.literal: In autumn people of the Korean states of the eastern Yi led their hosts to Lelang and submitted as subjects.
translations.idiomatic: Eastern Yi Koreans crossed to Lelang in a body to submit.

sentences.zh: 〈東夷有辰韓、卞韓、馬韓，謂之三韓國也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Among the eastern Yi are Chenhan, Bianhan, and Mahan; they are called the three Korean states.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The “three Han” confederacies are meant.〉

sentences.zh: 冬十月，東巡狩。

translations.literal: In the tenth month of winter he toured east.
translations.idiomatic: An eastern progress opened in the tenth month.

sentences.zh: 甲午，幸魯，進幸東海、楚、沛國。

translations.literal: On jiawu he visited Lu, advanced to the kingdoms of Donghai, Chu, and Pei.
translations.idiomatic: He sacrificed at Lu and toured the eastern princedoms.

sentences.zh: 十二月，匈奴寇天水。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month the Xiongnu raided Tianshui.
translations.idiomatic: Tianshui took another Xiongnu raid in the twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 壬寅，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On renyin the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: He closed the tour on renyin.

sentences.zh: 是歲，省五原郡，徙其吏人置河東。

translations.literal: That year Wuyuan commandery was abolished and its clerks and people were settled in Hedong.
translations.idiomatic: Wuyuan was struck from the map and its population moved into Hedong.

sentences.zh: 復濟陽縣傜役六歲。

translations.literal: Jiyang county had corvée remitted for six years.
translations.idiomatic: Jiyang enjoyed a six-year corvée holiday as the emperor’s birthplace.

sentences.zh: 二十一年春正月，武威將軍劉尚破益州夷，平之。

translations.literal: In the twenty-first year, in the first month of spring, general Liu Shang styled Weiwu broke the Yi of Yi province and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Shang finished the Yi campaign in the twenty-first year’s first month.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，安定屬國胡叛，屯聚青山，

translations.literal: In the fourth month of summer the Hu of Anding dependent state rebelled and massed at Qing Mountain,
translations.idiomatic: Dependent-state Hu in Anding gathered on Qing Mountain that fourth month,

sentences.zh: 〈青山在今慶州馬嶺縣西北。〉

translations.literal: 〈Qing Mountain is in what is now northwest of Maling county in Qing prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The ridge lay on the Anding–Bingzhou edge.〉

sentences.zh: 遣將兵長史陳訢討平之。

translations.literal: and chief clerk commanding troops Chen Xin was sent to campaign and pacify them.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Xin marched as chief clerk with troops and crushed them.

sentences.zh: 〈訢音欣。〉

translations.literal: 〈Xin is pronounced like xin (joy).〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The name Xin is read like the word for joy.〉

sentences.zh: 秋，鮮卑寇遼東，遼東太守祭肜大破之。

translations.literal: In autumn the Xianbei raided Liaodong; grand administrator of Liaodong Zhai Rong inflicted a great defeat on them.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Rong shattered a Xianbei raid on Liaodong that autumn.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，遣伏波將軍馬援出塞擊烏桓，不克。

translations.literal: In the tenth month of winter he sent wave-quelling general Ma Yuan beyond the barrier to strike the Wuhuan; he did not succeed.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan’s winter expedition against the Wuhuan failed to win a decisive victory.

sentences.zh: 匈奴寇上谷、中山。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu raided Shanggu and Zhongshan.
translations.idiomatic: Shanggu and Zhongshan saw Xiongnu incursions.

sentences.zh: 其冬，鄯善王、車師王等十六國皆遣子入侍奉獻，願請都護。

translations.literal: That winter the kings of Shanshan, Jushi, and sixteen other states all sent sons to attend court with tribute and begged a protector-general.
translations.idiomatic: Sixteen Western Region kings sent hostages and asked for a Han protector-general that winter.

sentences.zh: 〈都護，宣帝置，始以鄭吉為之，秩比二千石。

translations.literal: 〈The protector-general was established by Emperor Xuan; Zheng Ji was the first appointee, rank equivalent to two thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Western Regions post dated to Emperor Xuan and Zheng Ji.〉

sentences.zh: 都，緫也。

translations.literal: Du means overall;
translations.idiomatic: 〉Du meant overall command.〉

sentences.zh: 言緫護南北道。

translations.literal: it means overall protection of the south and north roads.
translations.idiomatic: 〉He coordinated both Silk Road corridors.〉

sentences.zh: 居烏壘城，察西域諸國動靜以聞。

translations.literal: He dwelt at Wulei city and inspected the movements of the various states of the Western Regions to report.
translations.idiomatic: 〉His headquarters at Wulei watched every petty king.〉

sentences.zh: 事見前書。〉

translations.literal: The affair is treated in the Former Han.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Former Han annals narrate the institution.〉

sentences.zh: 帝以中國初定，未遑外事，乃還其侍子，厚加賞賜。

translations.literal: The emperor, because the central lands were newly settled and he had no leisure for outer affairs, returned their attending sons and richly bestowed gifts.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu sent the hostages home with gifts but declined a new protector-general.

sentences.zh: 二十二年春閏月丙戌，幸長安，祠高廟，遂有事十一陵。

translations.literal: In the twenty-second year, on bingxu in the intercalary month of spring, he visited Chang’an, sacrificed at the lofty temple, then performed the rites at the eleven imperial tombs.
translations.idiomatic: The twenty-second year’s intercalary spring month brought another Chang’an round of shrines and tombs.

sentences.zh: 二月己巳，至自長安。

translations.literal: On jisi in the second month he returned from Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: He was back from Chang’an on jisi in the second month.

sentences.zh: 夏五月乙未晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On yiwei, the last day of the fifth month of summer, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: Summer ended with an eclipse on yiwei in the fifth month.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，司隷校尉蘇鄴下獄死。

translations.literal: In the seventh month of autumn colonel director of retainers Su Ye was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Su Ye perished in prison as colonel director of retainers.

sentences.zh: 九月戊辰，地震裂。

translations.literal: On wuchen in the ninth month the earth quaked and split.
translations.idiomatic: A violent earthquake struck on wuchen in the ninth month.

sentences.zh: 制詔曰：「日者地震，南陽尤甚。

translations.literal: An edict said: “Recently the earth quaked; Nanyang was especially severe.
translations.idiomatic: An edict opened: “The quake centered on Nanyang,

sentences.zh: 夫地者，任物至重，靜而不動者也。

translations.literal: The earth bears things to the utmost—it is heavy and ought not to move.
translations.idiomatic: The soil beneath the realm should lie still under the immense load it carries.

sentences.zh: 而今震裂，咎在君上。

translations.literal: Yet now it has cracked; the fault lies with the ruler.
translations.idiomatic: Its splitting laid blame at the throne.

sentences.zh: 鬼神不順無德，灾殃將及吏人，朕甚懼焉。

translations.literal: Spirits and gods do not favor the without-virtue; disaster will reach clerks and people—I am greatly afraid.
translations.idiomatic: The portent threatened every official and commoner in the afflicted zone.

sentences.zh: 其令南陽勿輸今年田租芻稾。

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that Nanyang not submit this year’s field rent or straw tax.
translations.idiomatic: He therefore waived the year’s grain and fodder levy for the entire Nanyang commandery.”

sentences.zh: 遣謁者案行，其死罪繫囚在戊辰以前，減死罪一等；

translations.literal: He sent petitioners-in-chief to go and inspect; as for prisoners bound for death held before wuchen, reduce death by one degree;
translations.idiomatic: Imperial agents toured the quake zone: capital convicts jailed before wuchen dropped one penalty notch,

sentences.zh: 徒皆［］解鉗，衣絲絮。

translations.literal: all convicts at labor had shackles removed and were allowed to wear silk wadding.
translations.idiomatic: labor gangs were unshackled and could wear padded silk against the cold.

translations.literal: 〈The graph means to loosen or release.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The verb meant stripping fetters.〉

sentences.zh: 倉頡篇曰：「鉗，釱也。

translations.literal: The Cangjie pian says: “Qian means fetters on the neck.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉Qian denoted the neck collar.〉

sentences.zh: 」音竒炎反。

translations.literal: Pronounced qi yan (fanqie).
translations.idiomatic: 〉Reading qi–yan.〉

sentences.zh: 前書音義曰：「釱，足鉗也。

translations.literal: The Yin Yi on the Former Han says: “Di means fetters on the feet.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉Di meant ankle irons.〉

sentences.zh: 」音徒計反，又大蓋反。

translations.literal: Pronounced tu ji (fanqie), also da gai (fanqie).
translations.idiomatic: 〉Fanqie variants tu–ji and da–gai are given.〉

sentences.zh: 舊法，在徒役者不得衣絲絮，今赦許之。〉

translations.literal: By old statute those serving labor punishment might not wear silk wadding; now the amnesty permits it.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Former law barred silk for convicts; the emergency edict waived that.〉

sentences.zh: 賜郡中居人壓死者棺錢，人三千。

translations.literal: He granted three thousand cash per person for coffins to residents of the commandery crushed to death.
translations.idiomatic: Families of the crushed dead received three thousand cash each toward burial.

sentences.zh: 其口賦逋稅而廬宅尤破壞者，勿收責。

translations.literal: As for those behind on poll tax and field tax whose houses were especially ruined, do not press collection.
translations.idiomatic: Back taxes were forgiven where homes were ruined beyond use.

sentences.zh: 〈漢儀注曰：「人年十五至五十六出賦錢，人百二十，為一筭。

translations.literal: 〈Han yi zhu says: “Persons aged fifteen to fifty-six pay poll cash, one hundred twenty cash each, one suan;
translations.idiomatic: 〉The note explains suan poll and child kou qian.〉

sentences.zh: 又七歲至十四出口錢，人二十，以供天子；

translations.literal: also from age seven to fourteen export cash, twenty each, to supply the Son of Heaven;
translations.idiomatic: 〉Children paid a smaller head tax.〉

sentences.zh: 至武帝時又口加三錢，以補車騎馬。

translations.literal: by Emperor Wu’s time three more cash per mouth were added to supply chariot and horse.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉Emperor Wu’s surcharge funded cavalry.〉

sentences.zh: 」逋稅謂欠田租也。〉

translations.literal: ”Bei shui means owing field rent.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Bei shui meant arrears on land tax.〉

sentences.zh: 吏人死亡，或在壞垣毀屋之下，而家羸弱不能收拾者，其以見錢穀取傭，為尋求之。」

translations.literal: Where clerks or people died perhaps under ruined walls and collapsed houses and the family is too weak to recover the bodies, use ready cash and grain to hire labor and search for them.”
translations.idiomatic: The court offered funds to hire recovery of bodies trapped in rubble.”

sentences.zh: 冬十月壬子，大司空朱浮免。

translations.literal: On renzi in the tenth month of winter grand minister of works Zhu Fu was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Fu left the ministry of works on renzi in winter.

sentences.zh: 癸丑，光祿勳杜林為大司空。

translations.literal: On guichou Minister of the Household Du Lin became grand minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: Du Lin succeeded him from the guangluxun post on guichou.

sentences.zh: 二十三年春正月，南郡蠻叛，遣武威將軍劉尚討破之，徙其種人於江夏。

translations.literal: In the twenty-third year, in the first month of spring, the Man of Nan commandery rebelled; general Liu Shang styled Weiwu was sent, defeated them, and moved their tribesmen to Jiangxia.
translations.idiomatic: Nan commandery tribes rose in the twenty-third year’s first month; Liu Shang crushed them and deported survivors to Jiangxia.

sentences.zh: 〈郡名，故城在今安州雲夢縣東南。〉

translations.literal: 〈Jiangxia is a commandery name; its old walled town is southeast of Yunmeng county in An prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Jiangxia lay east of the Yun marsh.〉

sentences.zh: 秋八月丙戌，大司空杜林薨。

translations.literal: On bingxu in the eighth month of autumn grand minister of works Du Lin died.
translations.idiomatic: Du Lin died in office on bingxu in the eighth month.

sentences.zh: 九月辛未，陳留太守玉況為大司徒。

translations.literal: On xinwei in the ninth month grand administrator of Chenliu Yu Kuang became grand minister of education.
translations.idiomatic: Yu Kuang of Chenliu took the grand steward seal on xinwei.

sentences.zh: 〈況字文伯，京兆人。

translations.literal: 〈Kuang’s style is Wenbo; he was a man of Jingzhao.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The memorialist identifies the appointee.〉

sentences.zh: 玉音肅。〉

translations.literal: The surname Yu is pronounced like su.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Yu here is read like su, not like “jade.”〉

sentences.zh: 冬十月丙申，太僕張純為大司空。

translations.literal: On bingshen in the tenth month of winter grand coachman Zhang Chun became grand minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Chun moved from grand coachman to grand minister of works on bingshen.

sentences.zh: 高句麗率種人詣樂浪內屬。

translations.literal: Gaogouli led its tribesmen to Lelang and submitted as subjects.
translations.idiomatic: The Gaogouli state presented itself at Lelang for incorporation.

sentences.zh: 十二月，武陵蠻叛，寇掠郡縣，遣劉尚討之，戰於沅水，

translations.literal: In the twelfth month the Man of Wuling rebelled, raided commanderies and counties; Liu Shang was sent against them and fought on the Yuan River,
translations.idiomatic: Wuling tribes raided that twelfth month; Liu Shang met them on the Yuan,

sentences.zh: 〈武陵，郡，今朗州也。

translations.literal: 〈Wuling commandery is modern Lang prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Wuling covered the Yuanzi basin.〉

sentences.zh: 沅，水名，出牂柯，東北過臨沅縣，至長沙入洞庭湖。〉

translations.literal: Yuan is a river name; it issues in Zangke, passes northeast through Linyuan county, and enters Dongting lake at Changsha.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Yuan’s course runs into Dongting.〉

sentences.zh: 尚軍敗歿。

translations.literal: and Shang’s army was defeated and he perished.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Shang’s column was destroyed and he fell in battle.

sentences.zh: 是歲，匈奴薁鞬日逐王比率部曲遣使詣西河內附。

translations.literal: That year the Worthy King of the Left Bi of the Xiongnu led his following and sent envoys to Xihe to submit as subjects.
translations.idiomatic: Bi, the Xiongnu Worthy King of the Left, sent envoys into Xihe to offer allegiance.

sentences.zh: 二十四年春正月乙亥，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the twenty-fourth year, on yihai in the first month of spring, there was a great amnesty for the realm.
translations.idiomatic: A general amnesty opened the twenty-fourth year on yihai.

sentences.zh: 匈奴薁鞬日逐王比遣使款五原塞，求扞禦北虜。

translations.literal: The Worthy King of the Left Bi of the Xiongnu sent envoys to treat at the Wuyuan barrier, begging to ward off the northern barbarians.
translations.idiomatic: Bi next sought Han backing against the northern court through the Wuyuan passes.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，武陵蠻寇臨沅，

translations.literal: In the seventh month of autumn the Wuling Man raided Linyuan,
translations.idiomatic: Wuling raiders struck Linyuan that seventh month,

sentences.zh: 〈縣名，屬武陵郡，故城在今朗州武陵縣。〉

translations.literal: 〈Linyuan is a county in Wuling commandery; its old walled town is in modern Wuling county, Lang prefecture.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The county sat on the middle Yuan.〉

sentences.zh: 遣謁者李嵩、中山太守馬成討蠻，不克，於是伏波將軍馬援率四將軍討之。

translations.literal: petitioner-in-chief Li Song and grand administrator of Zhongshan Ma Cheng were sent against the Man but did not prevail; thereupon wave-quelling general Ma Yuan leading four other generals was sent to campaign against them.
translations.idiomatic: Li Song and Ma Cheng failed; Ma Yuan took four generals south to finish the job.

sentences.zh: 詔有司申明舊制阿附蕃王法。

translations.literal: An edict ordered the offices to clarify anew the old statutes on currying favor with and attaching to vassal kings.
translations.idiomatic: The court reissued Former Han rules against faction with princes.

sentences.zh: 〈武帝時有淮南、衡山之謀，作左官之律，設附益之法。

translations.literal: 〈Under Emperor Wu the plots of Huainan and Hengyang produced the statutes on “left-hand officials” and the law against illicit enrichment.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Emperor Wu’s reaction to princely revolt framed the penalties.〉

sentences.zh: 前書音義曰：「人道尚右，言捨天子，仕諸侯為左官。

translations.literal: The Yin Yi on the Former Han says: “Among men the way esteems the right; saying one leaves the Son of Heaven to serve a feudal lord is a left-hand official.
translations.idiomatic: 〉“Left” meant morally deviant service under a king rather than the emperor.〉

sentences.zh: 左，僻也。

translations.literal: Left means deviant.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Left carried the sense of improper partisanship.〉

sentences.zh: 」阿曲附益王侯者，將有重法。

translations.literal: Those who crookedly attach and enrich princes will face heavy law.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Flatterers who enriched kings faced harsh sentences.〉

sentences.zh: 是為舊制，今更申明之。〉

translations.literal: That is the old system; now it is clarified anew.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Guangwu republished the bundle of prohibitions.〉

sentences.zh: 冬十月，匈奴薁鞬日逐王比自立為南單于，於是分為南、北匈奴。

translations.literal: In the tenth month of winter the Worthy King of the Left Bi of the Xiongnu set himself up as southern shanyu; thereby they split into southern and northern Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Bi declared southern shanyu in the tenth month, formally splitting the steppe.

sentences.zh: 二十五春正月，遼東徼外貊人

translations.literal: In the twenty-fifth year, in the first month of spring, the Mo people beyond the barrier of Liaodong
translations.idiomatic: Early in the twenty-fifth year Mo tribes beyond Liaodong,

sentences.zh: 〈貊人，穢貊國人也。

translations.literal: 〈Mo people are people of the Hui Mo states.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Mo here denotes Korean-border forest peoples.〉

sentences.zh: 貊音陌。〉

translations.literal: Mo is pronounced mo.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The syllable mo is read level mo.〉

sentences.zh: 寇右北平、漁陽、上谷、太原，遼東太守祭肜招降之。

translations.literal: raided Youbeiping, Yuyang, Shanggu, and Taiyuan; grand administrator of Liaodong Zhai Rong summoned and brought them to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: struck four commanderies until Zhai Rong talked them down.

sentences.zh: 烏桓大人來朝。

translations.literal: Great chiefs of the Wuhuan came to court.
translations.idiomatic: Wuhuan headmen presented themselves at Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 〈大人謂渠帥也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Great chief means chieftain.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉“Great chief” glosses the Wuhuan title for a chief.〉

sentences.zh: 南單于遣使詣闕貢獻，奉蕃稱臣；

translations.literal: The southern shanyu sent envoys to the palace with tribute, declaring himself a foreign vassal;
translations.idiomatic: The southern court sent tribute missions acknowledging vassalage;

sentences.zh: 又遣其左賢王擊破北匈奴，却地千餘里。

translations.literal: he also sent his Worthy King of the Left to attack and break the northern Xiongnu, pushing their territory back more than a thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: Bi’s left worthy shattered the northern wing and seized a thousand li of pasture.

sentences.zh: 三月，南單于遣子入侍。

translations.literal: In the third month the southern shanyu sent a son to attend court.
translations.idiomatic: A hostage prince arrived from the southern shanyu in the third month.

sentences.zh: 戊申晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On wushen, the last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The month closed with an eclipse on wushen.

sentences.zh: 伏波將軍馬援等破武陵蠻於臨沅。

translations.literal: Wave-quelling general Ma Yuan and others defeated the Wuling Man at Linyuan.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Yuan broke the Wuling host at Linyuan.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，叛蠻悉降。

translations.literal: In the tenth month of winter the rebel Man all surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: By winter’s tenth month the last Wuling bands capitulated.

sentences.zh: 夫餘王遣使奉獻。

translations.literal: The king of Puyo sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: The Puyo king in the Korean east sent gifts.

sentences.zh: 〈夫餘國在海東，去玄菟千里餘。〉

translations.literal: 〈The state of Puyo lies east of the sea, more than a thousand li from Xuantu.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Puyo lay beyond the Bohai rim from Xuantu.〉

sentences.zh: 是歲，烏桓大人率衆內屬，詣闕朝貢。

translations.literal: That year Wuhuan great chiefs led their hosts to submit as subjects and came to court with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Wuhuan magnates led their people in and brought tribute that year.

sentences.zh: 二十六年正月，詔有司增百官奉。

translations.literal: In the twenty-sixth year, in the first month, an edict ordered the offices to increase the salaries of the hundred officials.
translations.idiomatic: Official pay scales rose empire-wide in the twenty-sixth year’s first month.

sentences.zh: 〈續漢志曰：「大將軍、三公奉月三百五十斛，秩中二千石奉月百八十斛，二千石月百二十斛，比二千石月百斛，千石月九十斛，比千石月八十斛，六百石月七十斛，比六百石月五十五斛，四百石月五十斛，比四百石月四十五斛，三百石月四十斛，比三百石月三十七斛，二百石月三十斛，比二百石月二十七斛，百石月十六斛，斗食月十一斛，佐史月八斛。

translations.literal: 〈The Xu Han zhi records monthly grain salaries: grand general and the three dukes three hundred fifty hu; full two thousand shi rank one hundred eighty hu; two thousand shi one hundred twenty hu; comparable two thousand shi one hundred hu; one thousand shi ninety hu; comparable one thousand shi eighty hu; six hundred shi seventy hu; comparable six hundred shi fifty-five hu; four hundred shi fifty hu; comparable four hundred shi forty-five hu; three hundred shi forty hu; comparable three hundred shi thirty-seven hu; two hundred shi thirty hu; comparable two hundred shi twenty-seven hu; one hundred shi sixteen hu; dou shi eleven hu; assistant clerks eight hu.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Later Han gazetteer preserves the whole stepped scale of official stipends in hu of grain per month, from the summit of the bureaucracy down to minor clerks, with paired “comparable” grades at each rung.〉

sentences.zh: 凡諸受奉，錢穀各半。

translations.literal: In all who receive salary, cash and grain are each half.
