translations.idiomatic: 〉Typical stipends mixed grain and cash.〉

sentences.zh: 」奉音扶用反。〉

translations.literal: ”Feng is pronounced fu yong (fanqie).〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Feng (salary) reads fu–yong.〉

sentences.zh: 其千石已上，減於西京舊制；

translations.literal: For those at one thousand shi and above, reduce relative to the old Western capital system;
translations.idiomatic: High ranks took slightly less grain than Western Han had paid,

sentences.zh: 六百石已下，增於舊秩。

translations.literal: for those at six hundred shi and below, increase relative to the old rank.
translations.idiomatic: while junior posts received more than old tables.

sentences.zh: 初作壽陵。

translations.literal: He first began work on the longevity mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: Construction began on his own tomb, provisionally called the longevity mound.

sentences.zh: 〈初作陵未有名，故號壽陵，蓋取乆長之義也。

translations.literal: 〈When the tomb was first begun it had no name, hence it was called longevity mound, taking the sense of long endurance.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The working title echoed a wish for long reign.〉

sentences.zh: 漢自文帝以後皆預作陵，今循舊制也。〉

translations.literal: Since Han Wendi later rulers all prepared tombs in advance; now he follows the old pattern.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Western Han emperors had pre-built mausolea; Guangwu did the same.〉

sentences.zh: 將作大匠竇融上言園陵廣袤，無慮所用。

translations.literal: Chief architect for the palace Dou Rong submitted a statement on the breadth and length of the park and tomb, listing overall requirements.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong, chief builder, memorialized dimensions and total outlay for the necropolis.

sentences.zh: 〈前書曰：「將作少府，秦官，掌宮室，景帝改為大匠，秩二千石。

translations.literal: 〈The Former Han says: “Chief architect under the chamberlain for the palace, Qin office, in charge of palace chambers; Emperor Jing changed the title to chief architect, rank two thousand shi.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉The office dated to Qin palace works.〉

sentences.zh: 」說文曰：「南北曰袤，東西曰廣。

translations.literal: The Shuowen says: “From south to north is mao; from east to west is guang.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉Technical terms for longitudinal and lateral measure.〉

sentences.zh: 」廣雅曰：「無慮，都凡也。

translations.literal: The Guangya says: “Wu lu means the total reckoning.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉Wu lu meant aggregate figures.〉

sentences.zh: 」謂請園陵都凡制度也。

translations.literal: It means requesting the overall plan for the park and tomb.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Dou Rong asked for approval of the full layout.〉

sentences.zh: 袤音茂。〉

translations.literal: Mao is pronounced mao.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Mao rhymes with “mode.”〉

sentences.zh: 帝曰：「古者帝王之葬，皆陶人瓦器，木車茅馬，

translations.literal: The emperor said: “In antiquity the burials of emperors and kings all used pottery men and tiled vessels, wooden carts and thatched horses,
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu answered that ancient rulers used clay figurines, earthenware, straw horses,

sentences.zh: 〈禮曰：「塗車芻靈，自古有之。

translations.literal: 〈The Liji says: “Da carts and straw effigies have existed from antiquity.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Liji permitted simple grave goods.〉

sentences.zh: 」鄭玄注云：「芻靈，東茅為人馬也。」〉

translations.literal: ’Zheng Xuan’s note says: “Chu ling means binding straw into men and horses.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Straw effigies stood for chariot and escort.〉

sentences.zh: 使後世之人不知其處。

translations.literal: so that later generations would not know their location.
translations.idiomatic: so later ages could not find or rob the mound.

sentences.zh: 太宗識終始之義，景帝能述遵孝道，遭天下反覆，而霸陵獨完受其福，豈不美哉！

translations.literal: Taizong understood the meaning of finality; Emperor Jing was able to narrate and follow filial piety; though the realm turned upside down, Baling alone remained intact and received its blessing—how fine!
translations.idiomatic: He praised Wendi’s spare Baling tomb, untouched when the Red Eyebrows sacked Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 〈謂赤眉入長安，惟霸陵不掘。〉

translations.literal: 〈It means when the Red Eyebrows entered Chang’an only Baling was not dug up.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Only Wendi’s frugal mound escaped looting.〉

sentences.zh: 今所制地不過二三頃，無為山陵，陂池裁令流水而已。」

translations.literal: Now the land I mark out will not exceed two or three qing; do not make piled hills; ponds and pools only enough to let water flow—that is all.”
translations.idiomatic: His own plot would be a few qing with level sealing and modest pools—no mountain tomb.”

sentences.zh: 〈言不起山陵，裁令封土，陂池不停水而已。

translations.literal: 〈It means not raising a mountain tomb, only enough heaped earth and ponds that do not pond water.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The design forbade monumental earthworks.〉

sentences.zh: 陂音普何反。

translations.literal: Po is pronounced pu he (fanqie).
translations.idiomatic: 〉Po reads pu–he.〉

sentences.zh: 池音徒何反。〉

translations.literal: Chi is pronounced tu he (fanqie).〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Chi reads tu–he.〉

sentences.zh: 遣中郎將段郴授南單于璽綬，令入居雲中，

translations.literal: He sent leader of court gentlemen Duan Chen to invest the southern shanyu with seal and ribbon and ordered him to dwell in Yunzhong,
translations.idiomatic: Duan Chen carried the southern shanyu’s seal and installed him at Yunzhong,

sentences.zh: 〈郡名，在今勝州北。

translations.literal: 〈Yunzhong is a commandery name; it lies north of modern Sheng prefecture.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The seat sat on the bend north of the Yellow River.〉

sentences.zh: 郴音丑林反。〉

translations.literal: Chen is pronounced chou lin (fanqie).〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Duan Chen’s name reads chou–lin.〉

sentences.zh: 始置使匈奴中郎將，將兵衞護之。

translations.literal: For the first time the leader of court gentlemen for the Xiongnu was established, commanding troops to guard and protect him.
translations.idiomatic: Han created the Xiongnu gentleman-leader post with an escort force.

sentences.zh: 〈中郎將即段郴也。

translations.literal: 〈The leader of court gentlemen is Duan Chen.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Duan Chen doubled as first appointee.〉

sentences.zh: 漢官儀曰「使匈奴中郎將屯西河美稷縣」也。〉

translations.literal: The Han guan yi says: “The leader of court gentlemen for the Xiongnu was stationed at Meiji county in Xihe.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Han gazetteer places the escort headquarters at Meiji in Xihe commandery.〉

sentences.zh: 南單于遣子入侍，奉奏詣闕。

translations.literal: The southern shanyu sent a son to attend court and submitted memorials at the palace.
translations.idiomatic: The southern court sent a hostage and routine memorials.

sentences.zh: 於是雲中、五原、朔方、北地、定襄、鴈門、上谷、代八郡民歸於本土。

translations.literal: Thereupon the people of the eight commanderies Yunzhong, Wuyuan, Shuofang, Beidi, Dingxiang, Yanmen, Shanggu, and Dai returned to their native soil.
translations.idiomatic: The eight northern commanderies along the loop saw their people return from exile to the old garrison lands.

sentences.zh: 遣謁者分將施刑補理城郭。

translations.literal: He sent petitioners-in-chief separately leading relaxed-penalty convicts to repair city walls.
translations.idiomatic: Petitioners led commuted labor gangs to rebuild shattered walls.

sentences.zh: 〈施與弛同，解見上。〉

translations.literal: 〈Shi is the same as chi; explanation above.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Shi matched the “relaxed” labor amnesty above.〉

sentences.zh: 發遣邊民在中國者，布還諸縣，皆賜以裝錢，轉輸給食。

translations.literal: He issued and sent border people who were in the central lands back to their various counties, all granting travel cash and cartage of rations.
translations.idiomatic: The court paid the way for every displaced northerner sent back to a border county and supplied grain for the march.

sentences.zh: 〈東觀記曰：「時城郭丘墟，掃地更為，上悔前徙之。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Dong guan ji says: “At the time city walls were ruins and had to be rebuilt from bare ground; the emperor regretted the earlier removal.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The court admitted that prior evacuation had overstrained the frontier.〉

sentences.zh: 二十七年夏四月戊午，大司徒玉況薨。

translations.literal: In the twenty-seventh year, on wuwu in the fourth month of summer, grand minister of education Yu Kuang died.
translations.idiomatic: Yu Kuang died in office in the twenty-seventh year’s fourth month on wuwu.

sentences.zh: 五月丁丑，詔曰：「昔契作司徒，禹作司空，皆無『大』名，其令二府去『大』。」

translations.literal: On dingchou in the fifth month an edict said: “In antiquity Xie was steward of the masses and Yu was minister of works—neither had the word ‘grand’ in the title; let the two offices drop ‘grand.’”
translations.idiomatic: On dingchou he restored classical short titles, stripping “grand” from the two civil ministers.

sentences.zh: 〈朱祐奏冝令三公並去「大」名，以法經典，帝從其議。〉

translations.literal: 〈Zhu You memorialized that all three dukes should drop the word ‘grand’ to accord with the classics; the emperor followed the proposal.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Zhu You’s memorial drove the archaizing reform.〉

sentences.zh: 又改大司馬為太尉。

translations.literal: He also changed great marshal to grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Great marshal became grand commandant (taiwei).

sentences.zh: 驃騎大將軍行大司馬劉隆即日罷，太僕趙憙為太尉，大司農馮勤為司徒。

translations.literal: General of agile cavalry acting as great marshal Liu Long was dismissed the same day; grand coachman Zhao Xi became grand commandant, minister of agriculture Feng Qin steward of the masses.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Long stepped down the same day; Zhao Xi became taiwei and Feng Qin took the steward post.

sentences.zh: 益州郡徼外蠻夷率種人內屬。

translations.literal: Beyond the barrier of Yi province commandery barbarian Yi led their tribesmen to submit as subjects.
translations.idiomatic: Tribes outside Yi commandery offered allegiance that year.

sentences.zh: 北匈奴遣使詣武威乞和親。

translations.literal: The northern Xiongnu sent envoys to Wuwei begging for peace through marriage.
translations.idiomatic: The northern court sent a mission to Wuwei seeking heqin.

sentences.zh: 〈武威，郡，故城在今涼州姑臧縣西北，故涼城是也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Wuwei is a commandery; its old walled town is northwest of Guzang county in Liang prefecture today—the old Liang city.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Wuwei was the Hexi corridor hub at Guzang.〉

sentences.zh: 冬，魯王興、齊王石始就國。

translations.literal: In winter King Xing of Lu and King Shi of Qi first went to their fiefs.
translations.idiomatic: Kings Liu Xing and Liu Shi took up residence in their kingdoms that winter.

sentences.zh: 二十八年春正月己巳，徙魯王興為北海王，以魯國益東海。

translations.literal: In the twenty-eighth year, on jisi in the first month of spring, King Xing of Lu was moved to be king of Beihai, adding the territory of Lu to Donghai.
translations.idiomatic: On jisi in the twenty-eighth year’s first month Liu Xing shifted to Beihai while Lu lands enlarged Donghai.

sentences.zh: 賜東海王彊虎賁、旄頭、鍾虡之樂。

translations.literal: He granted King Qiang of Donghai tiger guard escort, mao tou vanguard, and bell-stand music.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Qiang received an imperial guard detachment, plume vanguard, and bronze bell orchestras.

sentences.zh: 〈漢官儀曰：「虎賁千五百人，戴鶡尾，屬虎賁中郎將。

translations.literal: 〈The Han guan yi says: “Fifteen hundred tiger guards wear pheasant tails and belong to the leader of court gentlemen for tiger guard.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉The tiger guard was a palace corps.〉

sentences.zh: 」又云：「舊選羽林為旄頭，被髮先駈。

translations.literal: ”It also says: “Formerly picked feather-forest men served as mao tou, with loosened hair riding in advance.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉Mao tou were shock riders with disheveled hair.〉

sentences.zh: 」魏文帝列異傳曰：「秦文公時梓樹化為牛，以騎擊之，騎不勝，或墮地髻解被髮，牛畏之，入水，故秦因是置旄頭騎，使先駈。

translations.literal: ”Emperor Wen of Wei’s Lie yi zhuan says: “When Duke Wen of Qin a catalpa turned into an ox; riders struck it but could not overcome it; some fell, their hairpins came loose and hair flew free; the ox feared that and entered the water; hence Qin for that reason established mao tou riders to ride in advance.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉A legend linked the mao tou unit to Qin omens.〉

sentences.zh: 」爾雅曰：「木謂之虡。

translations.literal: ”The Erya says: “Wood framework is called ju.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉Ju named the wooden frame.〉

sentences.zh: 」所以懸鍾磬也。

translations.literal: It is what is used to suspend bells and lithophones.
translations.idiomatic: 〉It held the bell array.〉

sentences.zh: 說文曰：「虡飾為猛獸。」〉

translations.literal: The Shuowen says: “The ju stand is carved in the shape of fierce beasts.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Carved beast legs supported the bells.〉

sentences.zh: 夏六月丁卯，沛太后郭氏薨，因詔郡縣捕王侯賔客，坐死者數千人。

translations.literal: On dingmao in the sixth month of summer Empress Dowager Guo of Pei died; thereupon an edict ordered commanderies and counties to arrest guests of kings and marquises; several thousand died under sentence.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Guo’s death triggered a purge of princely retainers that killed thousands by dingmao in summer.

sentences.zh: 〈時更始子鯉因沛獻王輔殺劉盆子兄恭，故王侯賔客多坐死。〉

translations.literal: 〈At the time Liu Li, son of Gengshi, through Prince Xian of Pei Fu had killed Liu Gong, elder brother of Liu Penzi; hence many guests of kings and marquises were condemned to death.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Court politics tied the purge to Liu Penzi’s kin.〉

sentences.zh: 秋八月戊寅，東海王彊、沛王輔、楚王英、濟南王康、淮陽王延始就國。

translations.literal: On wuyin in the eighth month of autumn King Qiang of Donghai, King Fu of Pei, King Ying of Chu, King Kang of Jinan, and King Yan of Huaiyang first went to their fiefs.
translations.idiomatic: Five royal sons entered their domains on wuyin in the eighth month.

sentences.zh: 冬十月癸酉，詔死罪繫囚皆一切募下蠶室，

translations.literal: On guiyou in the tenth month of winter an edict ordered all prisoners bound for death without exception to be recruited to the silkworm chamber,
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou an edict commuted male capital convicts to castration in the silkworm chamber,

sentences.zh: 〈蠶室，宮刑獄名。

translations.literal: 〈The silkworm chamber is the name of the palace punishment prison.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The heated cell got its name from silkworm rearing.〉

sentences.zh: 宮刑者畏風，須暖，作窨室蓄火如蠶室，因以名焉。

translations.literal: Those undergoing palace punishment fear wind and need warmth; they make a vaulted chamber storing fire like a silkworm room, hence the name.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Warmth prevented death after mutilation.〉

sentences.zh: 窨音一禁反。

translations.literal: Yin is pronounced yi jin (fanqie).
translations.idiomatic: 〉Yin reads yi–jin.〉

sentences.zh: 見前書音義。〉

translations.literal: See the Yin Yi on the Former Han.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Former Han jurisprudence defined the site.〉

sentences.zh: 其女子宮。

translations.literal: the women underwent palace confinement.
translations.idiomatic: women facing death suffered internal “palace” confinement instead.

sentences.zh: 〈謂幽閉也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Meaning confinement and seclusion.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉A non-mutilating substitute for women.〉

sentences.zh: 北匈奴遣使貢獻，乞和親。

translations.literal: The northern Xiongnu sent envoys with tribute, begging for peace through marriage.
translations.idiomatic: Northern envoys returned with gifts and another plea for heqin.

sentences.zh: 二十九年春二月丁巳朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: In the twenty-ninth year, on dingsi at the new moon of the second month of spring, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The second month opened with an eclipse on dingsi.

sentences.zh: 遣使者舉冤獄，出繫囚。

translations.literal: He sent envoys to investigate unjust jails and release prisoners.
translations.idiomatic: Inspectors reviewed prisons and freed the wrongly held.

sentences.zh: 庚申，賜天下男子爵，人二級；

translations.literal: On gengshen he granted noble rank to the men of the realm, two degrees each;
translations.idiomatic: On gengshen every male commoner received two steps of rank,

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤24e07、貧不能自存者粟，人五斛。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, the gravely infirm, and the poor unable to survive, grain, five hu each.
translations.idiomatic: while the needy categories received five hu of grain each.

sentences.zh: 夏四月乙丑，詔令天下繫囚自殊死已下及徒各減本罪一等，其餘贖罪輸作各有差。

translations.literal: On yichou in the fourth month of summer an edict ordered all prisoners in the realm from death by dismemberment downward and convicts at labor each to have their original crime reduced one degree; the rest who ransomed crimes or were sentenced to labor had graded differences.
translations.idiomatic: Yichou brought a blanket sentence reduction and tiered ransom for lesser crimes.

sentences.zh: 三十年春正月，鮮卑大人內屬，朝賀。

translations.literal: In the thirtieth year, in the first month of spring, Xianbei great chiefs submitted as subjects and came to court with congratulations.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei chiefs formally entered Han allegiance in the thirtieth year’s first month.

sentences.zh: 二月，東巡狩。

translations.literal: In the second month he toured east.
translations.idiomatic: He opened an eastern tour in the second month.

sentences.zh: 甲子，幸魯，進幸濟南。

translations.literal: On jiazi he visited Lu, advanced to Jinan.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi he sacrificed at Lu and continued to Jinan.

sentences.zh: 閏月癸丑，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On guichou in the intercalary month the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: He closed the tour on guichou in the intercalary month.

sentences.zh: 有星孛于紫宮。

translations.literal: There was a broom star in the Purple Palace asterism.
translations.idiomatic: A comet blazed through the Ziwei enclosure.

sentences.zh: 夏四月戊子，徙左翊王焉為中山王。

translations.literal: On wuzi in the fourth month of summer King Yan of Zuoyi was moved to be king of Zhongshan.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yan moved from Zuoyi to Zhongshan king on wuzi.

sentences.zh: 五月，大水。

translations.literal: In the fifth month there was great flooding.
translations.idiomatic: Floods struck in the fifth month.

sentences.zh: 賜天下男子爵，人二級；

translations.literal: He granted noble rank to the men of the realm, two degrees each;
translations.idiomatic: Another grant of two noble ranks per male followed,

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤［］、貧不能自存者粟，人五斛。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, the gravely infirm, and the poor unable to survive, grain, five hu each.
translations.idiomatic: with the same needy categories receiving five hu.

sentences.zh: 秋七月丁酉，幸魯國。

translations.literal: On dingyou in the seventh month of autumn he visited the kingdom of Lu.
translations.idiomatic: He toured the Lu kingdom on dingyou in the seventh month.

sentences.zh: 復濟陽縣是年傜役。

translations.literal: Jiyang county had corvée remitted for that year.
translations.idiomatic: Jiyang’s corvée was waived for the year of the tour.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月丁酉，至自魯。

translations.literal: On dingyou in the eleventh month of winter he returned from Lu.
translations.idiomatic: He was back from Lu on dingyou in the eleventh month.

sentences.zh: 三十一年夏五月，大水。

translations.literal: In the thirty-first year, in the fifth month of summer, there was great flooding.
translations.idiomatic: Summer floods returned in the thirty-first year’s fifth month.

sentences.zh: 戊辰，賜天下男子爵，人二級；

translations.literal: On wuchen he granted noble rank to the men of the realm, two degrees each;
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen another two-rank grant went to every male,

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤［］、貧不能自存者粟，人六斛。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, the gravely infirm, and the poor unable to survive, grain, six hu each.
translations.idiomatic: while grain for the needy rose to six hu.

sentences.zh: 癸酉晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On guiyou, the last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The month ended with an eclipse on guiyou.

sentences.zh: 是夏，蝗。

translations.literal: That summer there were locusts.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts swarmed that summer.

sentences.zh: 秋九月甲辰，詔令死罪繫囚皆一切募下蠶室，其女子宮。

translations.literal: On jiachen in the ninth month of autumn an edict ordered all prisoners bound for death without exception to be recruited to the silkworm chamber; the women underwent palace confinement.
translations.idiomatic: Jiachen repeated the castration commutation for men and confinement for women.

sentences.zh: 是歲，陳留雨穀，形如稗實。

translations.literal: That year in Chenliu grain rained down, shaped like barnyard grass seed.
translations.idiomatic: Chenliu reported grain-shaped objects falling like rain.

sentences.zh: 〈杜預注左傳云：「稗，草之似穀者。

translations.literal: 〈Du Yu’s note on the Zuozhuan says: “Bai is a grass resembling grain.”
translations.idiomatic: 〉Bai is a weed mimicking grain kernels.〉

sentences.zh: 」音蒲懈反。〉

translations.literal: Pronounced pu xie (fanqie).〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Reading pu–xie.〉

sentences.zh: 北匈奴遣使奉獻。

translations.literal: The northern Xiongnu sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: Northern envoys brought tribute again.

sentences.zh: 三月戊辰，司空張純薨。

translations.literal: On wuchen in the third month minister of works Zhang Chun died.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Chun died as minister of works on wuchen in the third month.

sentences.zh: 夏四月癸酉，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On guiyou in the fourth month of summer the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: The court returned to Luoyang on guiyou in the fourth month.

sentences.zh: 己卯，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On jimao there was a great amnesty for the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Jimao brought a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 復嬴、博、梁父、奉高，

translations.literal: Ying, Bo, Liangfu, and Fenggao were granted remission;
translations.idiomatic: Four counties on Mount Tai’s foot were favored,

sentences.zh: 〈四縣屬太山郡，故城在今兖州博城縣界。〉

translations.literal: 〈The four counties belonged to Taishan commandery; their old sites lie in the borders of Bocheng county in Yan prefecture today.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉They lay at the eastern sacred peak.〉

sentences.zh: 勿出今年田租芻稾。

translations.literal: do not submit this year’s field rent or straw tax.
translations.idiomatic: and owed no grain or fodder tax that year.

sentences.zh: 改年為中元。

translations.literal: The era name was changed to Zhongyuan.
translations.idiomatic: The reign was renamed Zhongyuan.

sentences.zh: 行幸長安。

translations.literal: He traveled to Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: He journeyed to Chang’an.

sentences.zh: 戊子，祀長陵。

translations.literal: On wuzi he sacrificed at Changling.
translations.idiomatic: On wuzi he offered at Gaozu’s Changling.

sentences.zh: 五月乙丑，至自長安。

translations.literal: On yichou in the fifth month he returned from Chang’an.
translations.idiomatic: He completed the western tour on yichou in the fifth month.

sentences.zh: 六月辛卯，太僕馮魴為司空。

translations.literal: On xinmao in the sixth month grand coachman Feng Fang became minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Fang took the ministry of works from grand coachman on xinmao.

sentences.zh: 乙未，司徒馮勤薨。

translations.literal: On yiwei steward of the masses Feng Qin died.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Qin died as steward on yiwei.

sentences.zh: 是夏，京師醴泉涌出，

translations.literal: That summer sweet springs welled up in the capital;
translations.idiomatic: Sweet springs bubbled up at Luoyang that summer,

sentences.zh: 〈尚書中候曰「俊乂在官，則醴泉出」也。〉

translations.literal: 〈The Shang shu zhong hou says: “When worthy men are in office, sweet springs issue.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The classic omen matched virtuous rule.〉

sentences.zh: 飲之者固疾皆愈，惟眇、蹇者不瘳。

translations.literal: those who drank them found chronic ailments all healed, only the blind and lame were not cured.
translations.idiomatic: drinkers claimed cures for chronic ills except the blind and crippled.

sentences.zh: 又有赤草生於水崖。

translations.literal: There was also red grass growing on the water’s bank.
translations.idiomatic: Red “auspicious” grass sprouted on riverbanks.

sentences.zh: 〈赤草，朱草也。

translations.literal: 〈Red grass is zhu grass.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Zhu grass was the crimson omen plant.〉

sentences.zh: 大戴禮曰：「朱草日生一葉，至十五日已後日落一葉，周而復始。」〉

translations.literal: The Da dai li says: “Zhu grass grows one leaf a day; after the fifteenth day it drops one leaf a day, cycling again.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Its daily leaf cycle was thought miraculous.〉

sentences.zh: 郡國頻上甘露。

translations.literal: Commanderies and kingdoms repeatedly reported sweet dew.
translations.idiomatic: Localities kept forwarding sweet-dew memorials.

sentences.zh: 羣臣奏言：「地祇靈應而朱草萌生。

translations.literal: The ministers submitted a statement: “Earth’s spirits answered with numinous signs and zhu grass sprouted.
translations.idiomatic: Ministers argued that earth portents proved virtue,

sentences.zh: 〈孝經援神契曰：「德至草木，即朱草生。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Xiaojing yuan shen qi says: “When virtue reaches to plants and trees, then zhu grass grows.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The chenwei text tied red grass to moral power.〉

sentences.zh: 孝宣帝每有嘉瑞，輒以改元，神爵、五鳳、甘露、黃龍，列為年紀，蓋以感致神祇，表彰德信。

translations.literal: Whenever filial Emperor Xuan had auspicious omens he changed the era name—Shenjue, Wufeng, Ganlu, Huanglong—listed as year records, thereby moving spirits and gods and displaying virtue and good faith.
translations.idiomatic: They cited Xuandi’s practice of renaming the reign for each major omen.

sentences.zh: 是以化致升平，稱為中興。

translations.literal: Therefore his transformation reached rising peace and he was called the mid-restoration.
translations.idiomatic: That policy had advertised the so-called “Zhongxing” age.

sentences.zh: 今天下清寧，靈物仍降。

translations.literal: Now the realm is clear and tranquil and numinous things continue to descend.
translations.idiomatic: They said Guangwu’s peace likewise drew omens.

sentences.zh: 陛下情存損挹，推而不居，豈可使祥符顯慶，沒而無聞？

translations.literal: Your Majesty’s mind holds modest self-restraint and you defer and do not claim them—how can we let auspicious tokens and manifest blessings sink without being heard?
translations.idiomatic: Yet the emperor refused to boast of heaven’s praise.

sentences.zh: 冝令太史撰集，

translations.literal: It is fitting to order the grand scribe to compile and collect them,
translations.idiomatic: They begged the grand scribe to compile a register,

sentences.zh: 〈太史，史官之長也。

translations.literal: 〈The grand scribe is the chief of the historiographers.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The grand scribe headed the archive.〉

sentences.zh: 前書音義曰：「太史公，武帝置，位在丞相之上。」〉

translations.literal: The Yin Yi on the Former Han says: “Grand scribe gong was established by Emperor Wu, rank above the chancellor.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Former Han briefly elevated the post above the chancellor.〉

sentences.zh: 以傳來世。

translations.literal: to transmit to later generations.
translations.idiomatic: for transmission to posterity.”

sentences.zh: 」帝不納。

translations.literal: ”The emperor did not accept.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu rejected the memorial.”

sentences.zh: 常自謙無德，每郡國所上，輒抑而不當，故史官罕得記焉。

translations.literal: He always called himself without virtue; whenever commanderies and kingdoms submitted reports he always suppressed them and did not confirm them, hence the historiographers rarely got to record them.
translations.idiomatic: He routinely dismissed local prodigy reports, so little entered the official annals.

sentences.zh: 秋，郡國三蝗。

translations.literal: In autumn locusts struck three commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Three commanderies suffered locusts that autumn.

sentences.zh: 冬十月辛未，司隷校尉東萊李訢為司徒。

translations.literal: On xinwei in the tenth month of winter colonel director of retainers Li Xin of Donglai became steward of the masses.
translations.idiomatic: Li Xin rose from colonel director of retainers to steward on xinwei.

sentences.zh: 甲申，使司空告祠高廟曰：「高皇帝與羣臣約，非劉氏不王。

translations.literal: On jiashen he sent the minister of works to announce sacrifice at the lofty temple, saying: “The High Ancestor made a covenant with the ministers: none but Liu may be king.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen the minister of works proclaimed at Gaozu’s temple the ban on non-Liu kings,

sentences.zh: 呂太后賊害三趙，

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Lu cruelly murdered the three Zhao princes,
translations.idiomatic: citing Lü’s murder of Liu princes of Zhao,

sentences.zh: 〈謂高帝子趙幽王友、趙恭王恢、趙隱王如意。〉

translations.literal: 〈Meaning Emperor Gao’s sons Zhao Prince You the Secluded, Zhao Prince Hui the Reverent, and Zhao Prince Yin Ruyi.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The three young sons of Gaozu are named.〉

sentences.zh: 專王呂氏，賴社稷之靈，祿、產伏誅，

translations.literal: and set up the Lü clan as kings; relying on the spirits of soil and grain, Lu and Chan were executed.
translations.idiomatic: until Zhou Bo destroyed Lü Lu and Lü Chan.

sentences.zh: 〈呂產、呂祿，並呂后兄弟子。

translations.literal: 〈Lü Chan and Lü Lu were both nephews of Empress Lü’s brothers.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Both were Lü affines holding army posts.〉

sentences.zh: 呂后崩，各擁南北軍，欲為亂，周勃、陳平等誅之。〉

translations.literal: When Empress Lü died each held the northern or southern armies and wished to rebel; Zhou Bo, Chen Ping, and others executed them.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Zhou and Chen ended the Lü coup.〉

sentences.zh: 天命幾墜，危朝更安。

translations.literal: Heaven’s mandate nearly fell; the endangered court was restored to peace.
translations.idiomatic: The Liu house had nearly fallen but recovered.

sentences.zh: 呂太后不冝配食高廟，同祧至尊。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Lü ought not to share sacrifice at the lofty temple or share the supreme shrine.
translations.idiomatic: Lü should no longer receive joint cult with Gaozu.

sentences.zh: 薄太后母德慈仁，

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Bo’s motherly virtue was kind and humane,
translations.idiomatic: Lady Bo’s maternal virtue deserved honor instead,

sentences.zh: 〈薄太后，高帝姬，孝文帝之母。〉

translations.literal: 〈Empress Dowager Bo was a consort of Emperor Gao and mother of Emperor Wen.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉She was Wendi’s mother.〉

sentences.zh: 孝文皇帝賢明臨國，子孫賴福，延祚至今。

translations.literal: Emperor Wen was wise and enlightened in ruling the state; descendants rely on his blessing, extending the mandate to the present.
translations.idiomatic: Wendi’s reign secured the line down to Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 其上薄太后尊號曰高皇后，配食地祇。

translations.literal: Let her be given the exalted title Gao empress and share sacrifice to earth’s spirits.
translations.idiomatic: Bo was to be canonized as Gao empress and paired with earth worship.

sentences.zh: 遷呂太后廟主于園，四時上祭。」

translations.literal: Move Empress Dowager Lü’s temple tablet to the park and perform seasonal sacrifices.”
translations.idiomatic: Lü’s tablet would be relegated to a side park with seasonal offerings only.”

sentences.zh: 〈園謂塋域也，於中置寑。〉

translations.literal: 〈Park means the burial precinct; a shrine chamber is set within it.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The “park” was a lesser necropolis enclosure.〉

sentences.zh: 十一月甲子晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On jiazi, the last day of the eleventh month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The eleventh month closed with an eclipse on jiazi.

sentences.zh: 是歲，初起明堂、靈臺、辟雍，及北郊兆域。

translations.literal: That year work first began on the Bright Hall, Spirit Terrace, Biyong, and the north suburban ritual precinct.
translations.idiomatic: Foundations were laid for the Bright Hall, observatory, imperial academy, and northern suburban altar.

sentences.zh: 〈大戴禮云：「明堂者凡九室，一室有四戶八牖，三十六戶，七十二牖。

translations.literal: 〈The Da dai li says: “The Bright Hall has nine chambers in all; each chamber has four doors and eight windows—thirty-six doors, seventy-two windows in all.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The classic layout specified nine chambers.〉

sentences.zh: 以茅蓋上，上員下方。

translations.literal: It is thatched with straw on top; the top is round and the bottom square.
translations.idiomatic: The roof was to be thatched round above square below,

sentences.zh: 赤綴戶也，白綴牖也。

translations.literal: Red trims the doors; white trims the windows.
translations.idiomatic: Vermilion framed the doors and white the windows of the mingtang design.

sentences.zh: 」禮圖又曰：「建武三十一年，作明堂，上員下方。

translations.literal: ”The Li tu also says: “In the thirty-first year of Jianwu they built the Bright Hall; the top was round and the bottom square.
translations.idiomatic: 〉A Han ritual diagram dated the build to Jianwu 31 with the round-heaven, square-earth form.〉

sentences.zh: 十二堂法日辰。

translations.literal: Twelve halls match the day branches.
translations.idiomatic: Twelve side halls corresponded to the twelve double-hours.

sentences.zh: 九室法九州。

translations.literal: Nine chambers match the nine provinces.
translations.idiomatic: Nine inner chambers mapped the nine regions.

sentences.zh: 室八窗，八九七十二，法一時之王。

translations.literal: Each chamber has eight windows; eight times nine is seventy-two, matching the kings of each double-hour.
translations.idiomatic: Eight windows per cell times nine cells made seventy-two openings, matching temporal symbolism.

sentences.zh: 室有十二戶，法陰陽之數。

translations.literal: Each chamber has twelve doors, matching the numbers of yin and yang.
translations.idiomatic: Twelve doors per chamber echoed the twelve months of yin-yang alternation.〉

sentences.zh: 」胡伯始云：「古清廟蓋以茅，今蓋以瓦，下藉茅，存古制也。

translations.literal: ”Hu Boshi said: “The ancient pure shrine was thatched with straw; now it is tiled, with straw laid beneath—preserving the ancient system.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Hu Boshi noted tiles over straw as a compromise with antiquity.〉

sentences.zh: 」漢官儀曰：「明堂四面起土作塹，上作橋，塹中無水。

translations.literal: ”The Han guan yi says: “The Bright Hall raises earth on four sides to make a moat, with bridges on top; there is no water in the moat.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The mingtang sat in a dry ritual moat with bridging ramps.〉

sentences.zh: 明堂去平城門二里所，天子出，從平城門，先歷明堂，乃至郊祀。

translations.literal: The Bright Hall is about two li from Pingcheng gate; when the Son of Heaven goes out from Pingcheng gate he first passes the Bright Hall, then proceeds to suburban sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The emperor’s procession always called at the mingtang before northern suburb rites.〉

sentences.zh: 」又曰：「辟雍去明堂三百步。

translations.literal: ”It also says: “The Biyong is three hundred paces from the Bright Hall.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The ring-water academy stood a fixed distance from the mingtang.〉

sentences.zh: 車駕臨辟雍，從北門入。

translations.literal: When the imperial carriage visits the Biyong it enters from the north gate.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Imperial visits used the north entry.〉

sentences.zh: 三月、九月，皆於中行郷射禮。

translations.literal: In the third and ninth months the village archery rite was performed in the middle.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Spring and autumn archery assemblies met in the central court.〉

sentences.zh: 辟雍以水周其外，以節觀者。

translations.literal: The Biyong has water surrounding its outside to regulate spectators.
translations.idiomatic: 〉A moat kept the crowd at proper distance.〉

sentences.zh: 諸侯曰泮宮。

translations.literal: Feudal lords call it the Pan palace.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Regional schools were half-moated pan gong.〉

sentences.zh: 東西南有水，北無，下天子也。

translations.literal: There is water on east, south, and west, none on the north—below the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Han’s biyong left the north open as a sign of inferiority to the Son of Heaven.〉

sentences.zh: 」漢宮閣疏曰：「靈臺高三丈，十二門。

translations.literal: ”Han gong ge shu says: “The Spirit Terrace is three zhang high, with twelve gates.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The observatory rose three zhang with twelve gates.〉

sentences.zh: 天子曰靈臺，諸侯曰觀臺。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven calls it the Spirit Terrace; feudal lords call it the observation terrace.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Only the emperor’s tower bore the name ling tai.〉

sentences.zh: 」漢官儀：「北郊壇在城西北角，去城一里所。

translations.literal: ”Han guan yi: “The north suburban altar is at the northwest corner of the city, about one li from the wall.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The earth altar sat just outside the northwest wall.〉

sentences.zh: 為方壇四陛，但有壇祠舍而已。

translations.literal: It is a square altar with four stairways; there are only the altar and shrine buildings.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The precinct held a plain square mound and minimal buildings.〉

sentences.zh: 其鼓吹樂及舞人御帳，皆徙南郊之具。

translations.literal: The processional music, dancers, and imperial tents were all moved from the equipment of the southern suburb.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Regalia was borrowed from the southern rite to outfit the northern altar.〉

sentences.zh: 地祇位南面西上，高皇后配，西面，皆在壇上。

translations.literal: Earth’s spirit’s seat faces south, west priority; Gao empress is paired, facing west—both are on the altar.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Hou tu faced south with Lady Bo west as consort to the soil deity.〉

sentences.zh: 地理羣神從食壇下。

translations.literal: The host of terrestrial spirits partake below the altar.
translations.idiomatic: 〉Lesser earth gods received offerings below the main mound.〉

sentences.zh: 南郊焚犢，北郊埋犢。」〉

translations.literal: At the southern suburb the calf is burned; at the northern suburb the calf is buried.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉South burned the victim; north interred it—paired chthonic rites.”〉

sentences.zh: 宣布圖讖於天下。

translations.literal: He proclaimed the charts and weft texts throughout the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Chenwei prognostications were published empire-wide.

sentences.zh: 復濟陽、南頓是年傜役。

translations.literal: Jiyang and Nandun had corvée remitted for that year.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor’s two natal towns received another corvée holiday.

sentences.zh: 參狼羌寇武都，敗郡兵，隴西太守劉盱遣軍救之，及武都郡兵討叛羌，皆破之。

translations.literal: The Canlang Qiang raided Wudu, defeated commandery troops; grand administrator of Longxi Liu Xu sent troops to rescue them, and together with Wudu commandery troops campaigned against the rebel Qiang; all defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Canlang Qiang struck Wudu; Liu Xu of Longxi and Wudu forces combined to crush the revolt.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月辛未，初立北郊，祀后土。

translations.literal: In the second year, on xinwei in the first month of spring, the north suburban altar was first established and Hou tu was sacrificed to.
translations.idiomatic: On xinwei in Zhongyuan 2 the northern earth altar was inaugurated.

sentences.zh: 東夷倭奴國主遣使奉獻。

translations.literal: The lord of the Wa-nu state of the eastern Yi sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: The Wa ruler in Japan sent tribute across the sea.

sentences.zh: 〈倭在帶方東南大海中，依山島為國。〉

translations.literal: 〈Wa lies in the great sea southeast of Daifang, forming a state on mountain islands.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The note locates Wa beyond the Korean commandery coast.〉

sentences.zh: 二月戊戌，帝崩於南宮前殿，年六十二。

translations.literal: On wuxu in the second month the emperor died in the front hall of the southern palace, aged sixty-two.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu died in the southern palace forehall on wuxu in the second month at sixty-two.

sentences.zh: 〈伏侯古今注曰：「是歲在丁巳。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Fu Hou gu jin zhu says: “That year was in the ding si cycle.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉A chronology note fixes the sexagenary year.〉

sentences.zh: 遺詔曰：「朕無益百姓，皆如孝文皇帝制度，務從約省。

translations.literal: The testamentary edict said: “I have not benefited the hundred surnames; all shall follow the institutions of filial Emperor Wen, striving for restraint and reduction.
translations.idiomatic: His will demanded Wendi-style austerity for the funeral,

sentences.zh: 〈文帝葬皆以瓦器，不以金銀銅錫為飾，因其山，不起墳。〉

translations.literal: 〈Emperor Wen’s burial used only pottery vessels, not gold, silver, bronze, or tin for ornament; following the mountain, no mound was raised.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Wendi’s frugal mound on the natural hill was the model.〉

sentences.zh: 刺史、二千石長吏皆無離城郭，無遣吏及因郵奏。」

translations.literal: Inspectors and chief magistrates at two thousand shi must all not leave city walls; do not dispatch clerks or submit memorials via post stations.”
translations.idiomatic: Local officials were forbidden to leave their seats or flood the post roads with mourning traffic.”

sentences.zh: 〈說文曰：「郵，境上行書舍也。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Shuowen says: “You means relay stations for documents on the borders.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉You denoted the imperial courier network.〉

sentences.zh: 初，帝在兵閒乆，厭武事，且知天下疲秏，思樂息肩。

translations.literal: At first the emperor had long been amid armies, weary of military affairs, and knew the realm was exhausted and wished to rest shoulders from the load.
translations.idiomatic: Having soldiered for years, he sought peace and fiscal rest,

sentences.zh: 〈左傳曰：「息肩于晉。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Zuozhuan says: “Rest shoulders in Jin.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The idiom meant laying down a heavy burden.〉

sentences.zh: 自隴、蜀平後，非儆急，未甞復言軍旅。

translations.literal: After Long and Shu were pacified, unless there was urgent alarm he never again spoke of armies.
translations.idiomatic: After the western campaigns he avoided talk of war.

sentences.zh: 皇太子甞問攻戰之事，帝曰：「昔衞靈公問陳，孔子不對，此非爾所及。」

translations.literal: The heir apparent once asked about matters of attack and warfare; the emperor said: “In the past Duke Ling of Wei asked about battle formations; Confucius did not answer—this is not what concerns you.”
translations.idiomatic: When the crown prince pressed for military lore, he quoted Confucius’s refusal to discuss war with Duke Ling of Wei.

sentences.zh: 〈論語：「衞靈公問陳於孔子。

translations.literal: 〈The Analects: “Duke Ling of Wei asked Confucius about battle formations.
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Lunyu passage framed the rebuke.〉

sentences.zh: 曰：『俎豆之事，則甞聞之矣；

translations.literal: He said: “As for matters of sacrificial stands and vessels, I have heard something;
translations.idiomatic: 〉Confucius admitted ritual but not war.〉

sentences.zh: 軍旅之事，未之學也。』」〉

translations.literal: as for matters of armies, I have not studied them.’”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The canonical excuse closed the topic.”〉

sentences.zh: 每旦視朝，日仄乃罷。

translations.literal: Each dawn he viewed court and did not dismiss until the sun slanted.
translations.idiomatic: He held dawn audiences that ran until late afternoon.

sentences.zh: 數引公卿、郎、將講論經理，夜分乃寐。

translations.literal: He repeatedly led dukes, ministers, gentlemen of the palace, and generals to discuss the classics and their principles, and only at midnight did he sleep.
translations.idiomatic: Evenings were spent debating scripture with high officials until midnight.

sentences.zh: 〈分猶半也。〉

translations.literal: 〈Fen means half.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉“Night half” meant the middle of the night.〉

sentences.zh: 皇太子見帝勤勞不怠，承間諫曰：「陛下有禹湯之明，而失黃老養性之福，

translations.literal: When the heir apparent saw the emperor toiling without slack, he took an opening to remonstrate: “Your Majesty has the clarity of Yu and Tang, yet misses the blessing of nurturing life in the manner of Huang and Lao.
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince urged him to adopt Huang–Lao self-care,

sentences.zh: 〈黃帝、老子。〉

translations.literal: 〈Huang Di and Lao Dan.〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉Huang–Lao meant the Daoist sage-kings.〉

sentences.zh: 願頤愛精神，優游自寧。

translations.literal: I beg you to cherish your spirit and intelligence and roam at ease in quiet.”
translations.idiomatic: and to ease his pace for health.”

sentences.zh: 」帝曰：「我自樂此，不為疲也。

translations.literal: ”The emperor said: “I myself delight in this; I do not consider it weariness.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu answered that statecraft was his joy, not a drain.”

sentences.zh: 」雖身濟大業，兢兢如不及，故能明慎政體，緫［］權綱，量時度力，舉無過事。

translations.literal: ”Although he personally accomplished the great enterprise, he was fearful as if he could not keep up; therefore he could illuminate and be careful in the body of government, gather the cords of authority, measure the times and estimate strength, and in all undertakings commit no excess.
translations.idiomatic: The annalist praises his anxious care, measured policy, and firm grip on power.

sentences.zh: 退功臣而進文吏，戢弓矢而散馬牛，雖道未方古，斯亦止戈之武焉。

translations.literal: He retired meritorious warriors and promoted civil clerks, stored away bows and arrows and dispersed horses and oxen; although his way did not match antiquity, this too was the “martial” that stops spears.
translations.idiomatic: He demobilized the sword in favor of scribes—still a kind of true wu, stopping war by ruling well.
The historian’s judgment on Guangwu’s reign ends with this closing quotation mark.

sentences.zh: 〈左傳曰：「於文，止戈為武也。」〉

translations.literal: 〈The Zuozhuan says: “In the analysis of graphs, stopping spears is wu.”〉
translations.idiomatic: 〉The Zuozhuan etymology glosses wu as laying down arms.〉
